chapters.zh: 耶和华从会幕中呼叫摩西，对他说：
chapters.en: And the LORD called unto Moses, and spake unto him out of the tabernacle of the congregation, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：你们中间若有人献供物给耶和华，要从牛群羊群中献牲畜为供物。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If any man of you bring an offering unto the LORD, ye shall bring your offering of the cattle, of the herd, and of the flock.

chapters.zh: 他的供物若以牛为燔祭，就要在会幕门口献一只没有残疾的公牛，可以在耶和华面前蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: If his offering a burnt sacrifice of the herd, let him offer a male without blemish: he shall offer it of his own voluntary will at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他要按手在燔祭牲的头上，燔祭便蒙悦纳，为他赎罪。
chapters.en: And he shall put his hand upon the head of the burnt offering; and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement for him.

chapters.zh: 他要在耶和华面前宰公牛；亚伦子孙作祭司的，要奉上血，把血洒在会幕门口、坛的周围。
chapters.en: And he shall kill the bullock before the LORD: and the priests, Aaron's sons, shall bring the blood, and sprinkle the blood round about upon the altar that the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 那人要剥去燔祭牲的皮，把燔祭牲切成块子。
chapters.en: And he shall flay the burnt offering, and cut it into his pieces.

chapters.zh: 祭司亚伦的子孙要把火放在坛上，把柴摆在火上。
chapters.en: And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar, and lay the wood in order upon the fire:

chapters.zh: 亚伦子孙作祭司的，要把肉块和头并脂油摆在坛上火的柴上。
chapters.en: And the priests, Aaron's sons, shall lay the parts, the head, and the fat, in order upon the wood that on the fire which upon the altar:

chapters.zh: 但燔祭的脏腑与腿要用水洗。祭司就要把一切全烧在坛上，当作燔祭，献与耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: But his inwards and his legs shall he wash in water: and the priest shall burn all on the altar, a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人的供物若以绵羊或山羊为燔祭，就要献上没有残疾的公羊。
chapters.en: And if his offering of the flocks, , of the sheep, or of the goats, for a burnt sacrifice; he shall bring it a male without blemish.

chapters.zh: 要把羊宰于坛的北边，在耶和华面前；亚伦子孙作祭司的，要把羊血洒在坛的周围。
chapters.en: And he shall kill it on the side of the altar northward before the LORD: and the priests, Aaron's sons, shall sprinkle his blood round about upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 要把燔祭牲切成块子，连头和脂油，祭司就要摆在坛上火的柴上；
chapters.en: And he shall cut it into his pieces, with his head and his fat: and the priest shall lay them in order on the wood that on the fire which upon the altar:

chapters.zh: 但脏腑与腿要用水洗，祭司就要全然奉献，烧在坛上。这是燔祭，是献与耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: But he shall wash the inwards and the legs with water: and the priest shall bring all, and burn upon the altar: it a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人奉给耶和华的供物，若以鸟为燔祭，就要献斑鸠或是雏鸽为供物。
chapters.en: And if the burnt sacrifice for his offering to the LORD of fowls, then he shall bring his offering of turtledoves, or of young pigeons.

chapters.zh: 祭司要把鸟拿到坛前，揪下头来，把鸟烧在坛上；鸟的血要流在坛的旁边；
chapters.en: And the priest shall bring it unto the altar, and wring off his head, and burn on the altar; and the blood thereof shall be wrung out at the side of the altar:

chapters.zh: 又要把鸟的嗉子和脏物除掉，（脏物：或作翎毛）丢在坛的东边倒灰的地方。
chapters.en: And he shall pluck away his crop with his feathers, and cast it beside the altar on the east part, by the place of the ashes:

chapters.zh: 要拿著鸟的两个翅膀，把鸟撕开，只是不可撕断；祭司要在坛上、在火的柴上焚烧。这是燔祭，是献与耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And he shall cleave it with the wings thereof, shall not divide asunder: and the priest shall burn it upon the altar, upon the wood that upon the fire: it a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 若有人献素祭为供物给耶和华，要用细面浇上油，加上乳香，
chapters.en: And when any will offer a meat offering unto the LORD, his offering shall be fine flour; and he shall pour oil upon it, and put frankincense thereon:

chapters.zh: 带到亚伦子孙作祭司的那里；祭司就要从细面中取出一把来，并取些油和所有的乳香，然后要把所取的这些作为纪念，烧在坛上，是献与耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And he shall bring it to Aaron's sons the priests: and he shall take thereout his handful of the flour thereof, and of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof; and the priest shall burn the memorial of it upon the altar, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD:

chapters.zh: 素祭所剩的要归给亚伦和他的子孙；这是献与耶和华的火祭中为至圣的。
chapters.en: And the remnant of the meat offering Aaron's and his sons': a thing most holy of the offerings of the LORD made by fire.

chapters.zh: 若用炉中烤的物为素祭，就要用调油的无酵细面饼，或是抹油的无酵薄饼。
chapters.en: And if thou bring an oblation of a meat offering baken in the oven, unleavened cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.

chapters.zh: 若用铁鏊上做的物为素祭，就要用调油的无酵细面，
chapters.en: And if thy oblation a meat offering in a pan, it shall be fine flour unleavened, mingled with oil.

chapters.zh: 分成块子，浇上油；这是素祭。
chapters.en: Thou shalt part it in pieces, and pour oil thereon: it a meat offering.

chapters.zh: 若用煎盘做的物为素祭，就要用油与细面作成。
chapters.en: And if thy oblation a meat offering in the fryingpan, it shall be made fine flour with oil.

chapters.zh: 要把这些东西做的素祭带到耶和华面前，并奉给祭司，带到坛前。
chapters.en: And thou shalt bring the meat offering that is made of these things unto the LORD: and when it is presented unto the priest, he shall bring it unto the altar.

chapters.zh: 祭司要从素祭中取出作为纪念的，烧在坛上，是献与耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And the priest shall take from the meat offering a memorial thereof, and shall burn upon the altar: an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 素祭所剩的要归给亚伦和他的子孙。这是献与耶和华的火祭中为至圣的。
chapters.en: And that which is left of the meat offering Aaron's and his sons': a thing most holy of the offerings of the LORD made by fire.

chapters.zh: 凡献给耶和华的素祭都不可有酵；因为你们不可烧一点酵、一点蜜当作火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: No meat offering, which ye shall bring unto the LORD, shall be made with leaven: for ye shall burn no leaven, nor any honey, in any offering of the LORD made by fire.

chapters.zh: 这些物要献给耶和华作为初熟的供物，只是不可在坛上献为馨香的祭。
chapters.en: As for the oblation of the firstfruits, ye shall offer them unto the LORD: but they shall not be burnt on the altar for a sweet savour.

chapters.zh: 凡献为素祭的供物都要用盐调和，在素祭上不可缺了你神立约的盐。一切的供物都要配盐而献。
chapters.en: And every oblation of thy meat offering shalt thou season with salt; neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy God to be lacking from thy meat offering: with all thine offerings thou shalt offer salt.

chapters.zh: 若向耶和华献初熟之物为素祭，要献上烘了的禾穗子，就是轧了的新穗子，当作初熟之物的素祭。
chapters.en: And if thou offer a meat offering of thy firstfruits unto the LORD, thou shalt offer for the meat offering of thy firstfruits green ears of corn dried by the fire, corn beaten out of full ears.

chapters.zh: 并要抹上油，加上乳香；这是素祭。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put oil upon it, and lay frankincense thereon: it a meat offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司要把其中作为纪念的，就是一些轧了的禾穗子和一些油，并所有的乳香，都焚烧，是向耶和华献的火祭。
chapters.en: And the priest shall burn the memorial of it, of the beaten corn thereof, and of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof: an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人献供物为平安祭（平安：或作酬恩；下同），若是从牛群中献，无论是公的是母的，必用没有残疾的献在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And if his oblation a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offer of the herd; whether a male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他要按手在供物的头上，宰于会幕门口。亚伦子孙作祭司的，要把血洒在坛的周围。
chapters.en: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron's sons the priests shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.

chapters.zh: 从平安祭中，将火祭献给耶和华，也要把盖脏的脂油和脏上所有的脂油，
chapters.en: And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the LORD; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that upon the inwards,

chapters.zh: 并两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，就是靠腰两旁的脂油，与肝上的网子和腰子，一概取下。
chapters.en: And the two kidneys, and the fat that on them, which by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的子孙要把这些烧在坛的燔祭上，就是在火的柴上，是献与耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And Aaron's sons shall burn it on the altar upon the burnt sacrifice, which upon the wood that on the fire: an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人向耶和华献供物为平安祭，若是从羊群中献，无论是公的是母的，必用没有残疾的。
chapters.en: And if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offering unto the LORD of the flock; male or female, he shall offer it without blemish.

chapters.zh: 若献一只羊羔为供物，必在耶和华面前献上，
chapters.en: If he offer a lamb for his offering, then shall he offer it before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 并要按手在供物的头上，宰于会幕前。亚伦的子孙要把血洒在坛的周围。
chapters.en: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it before the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron's sons shall sprinkle the blood thereof round about upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 从平安祭中，将火祭献给耶和华，其中的脂油和整肥尾巴都要在靠近脊骨处取下，并要把盖脏的脂油和脏上所有的脂油，
chapters.en: And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the LORD; the fat thereof, the whole rump, it shall he take off hard by the backbone; and the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that upon the inwards,

chapters.zh: 两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，就是靠腰两旁的脂油，并肝上的网子和腰子，一概取下。
chapters.en: And the two kidneys, and the fat that upon them, which by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.

chapters.zh: 祭司要在坛上焚烧，是献给耶和华为食物的火祭。
chapters.en: And the priest shall burn it upon the altar: the food of the offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人的供物若是山羊，必在耶和华面前献上。
chapters.en: And if his offering a goat, then he shall offer it before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 要按手在山羊头上，宰于会幕前。亚伦的子孙要把血洒在坛的周围，
chapters.en: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of it, and kill it before the tabernacle of the congregation: and the sons of Aaron shall sprinkle the blood thereof upon the altar round about.

chapters.zh: 又把盖脏的脂油和脏上所有的脂油，两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，就是靠腰两旁的脂油，并肝上的网子和腰子，一概取下，献给耶和华为火祭。
chapters.en: And he shall offer thereof his offering, an offering made by fire unto the LORD; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that upon the inwards,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: And the two kidneys, and the fat that upon them, which by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.

chapters.zh: 祭司要在坛上焚烧，作为馨香火祭的食物。脂油都是耶和华的。
chapters.en: And the priest shall burn them upon the altar: the food of the offering made by fire for a sweet savour: all the fat the LORD'S.

chapters.zh: 在你们一切的住处，脂油和血都不可吃；这要成为你们世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: a perpetual statute for your generations throughout all your dwellings, that ye eat neither fat nor blood.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：若有人在耶和华所吩咐不可行的甚么事上误犯了一件，
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD which ought not to be done, and shall do against any of them:

chapters.zh: 或是受膏的祭司犯罪，使百姓陷在罪里，就当为他所犯的罪把没有残疾的公牛犊献给耶和华为赎罪祭。
chapters.en: If the priest that is anointed do sin according to the sin of the people; then let him bring for his sin, which he hath sinned, a young bullock without blemish unto the LORD for a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 他要牵公牛到会幕门口，在耶和华面前按手在牛的头上，把牛宰于耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And he shall bring the bullock unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD; and shall lay his hand upon the bullock's head, and kill the bullock before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 受膏的祭司要取些公牛的血带到会幕，
chapters.en: And the priest that is anointed shall take of the bullock's blood, and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation:

chapters.zh: 把指头蘸于血中，在耶和华面前对著圣所的幔子弹血七次，
chapters.en: And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle of the blood seven times before the LORD, before the vail of the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 又要把些血抹在会幕内、耶和华面前香坛的四角上，再把公牛所有的血倒在会幕门口、燔祭坛的脚那里。
chapters.en: And the priest shall put of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the LORD, which in the tabernacle of the congregation; and shall pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 要把赎罪祭公牛所有的脂油，乃是盖脏的脂油和脏上所有的脂油，
chapters.en: And he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that upon the inwards,

chapters.zh: 并两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，就是靠腰两旁的脂油，与肝上的网子和腰子，一概取下，
chapters.en: And the two kidneys, and the fat that upon them, which by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away,

chapters.zh: 与平安祭公牛上所取的一样；祭司要把这些烧在燔祭的坛上。
chapters.en: As it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 公牛的皮和所有的肉，并头、腿、脏、腑、粪，
chapters.en: And the skin of the bullock, and all his flesh, with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung,

chapters.zh: 就是全公牛，要搬到营外洁净之地、倒灰之所，用火烧在柴上。
chapters.en: Even the whole bullock shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place, where the ashes are poured out, and burn him on the wood with fire: where the ashes are poured out shall he be burnt.

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众若行了耶和华所吩咐不可行的甚么事，误犯了罪，是隐而未现，会众看不出来的，
chapters.en: And if the whole congregation of Israel sin through ignorance, and the thing be hid from the eyes of the assembly, and they have done any of the commandments of the LORD which should not be done, and are guilty;

chapters.zh: 会众一知道所犯的罪就要献一只公牛犊为赎罪祭，牵到会幕前。
chapters.en: When the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known, then the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin, and bring him before the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 会中的长老就要在耶和华面前按手在牛的头上，将牛在耶和华面前宰了。
chapters.en: And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the LORD: and the bullock shall be killed before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 受膏的祭司要取些公牛的血带到会幕，
chapters.en: And the priest that is anointed shall bring of the bullock's blood to the tabernacle of the congregation:

chapters.zh: 把指头蘸于血中，在耶和华面前对著幔子弹血七次，
chapters.en: And the priest shall dip his finger of the blood, and sprinkle seven times before the LORD, before the vail.

chapters.zh: 又要把些血抹在会幕内、耶和华面前坛的四角上，再把所有的血倒在会幕门口、燔祭坛的脚那里。
chapters.en: And he shall put of the blood upon the horns of the altar which before the LORD, that in the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall pour out all the blood at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 把牛所有的脂油都取下，烧在坛上；
chapters.en: And he shall take all his fat from him, and burn upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 收拾这牛，与那赎罪祭的牛一样。祭司要为他们赎罪，他们必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall do with the bullock as he did with the bullock for a sin offering, so shall he do with this: and the priest shall make an atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven them.

chapters.zh: 他要把牛搬到营外烧了，像烧头一个牛一样；这是会众的赎罪祭。
chapters.en: And he shall carry forth the bullock without the camp, and burn him as he burned the first bullock: it a sin offering for the congregation.

chapters.zh: 官长若行了耶和华 ─ 他神所吩咐不可行的甚么事，误犯了罪，
chapters.en: When a ruler hath sinned, and done through ignorance any of the commandments of the LORD his God which should not be done, and is guilty;

chapters.zh: 所犯的罪自己知道了，就要牵一只没有残疾的公山羊为供物，
chapters.en: Or if his sin, wherein he hath sinned, come to his knowledge; he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a male without blemish:

chapters.zh: 按手在羊的头上，宰于耶和华面前、宰燔祭牲的地方；这是赎罪祭。
chapters.en: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司要用指头蘸些赎罪祭牲的血，抹在燔祭坛的四角上，把血倒在燔祭坛的脚那里。
chapters.en: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out his blood at the bottom of the altar of burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 所有的脂油，祭司都要烧在坛上，正如平安祭的脂油一样。至于他的罪，祭司要为他赎了，他必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall burn all his fat upon the altar, as the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall make an atonement for him as concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 民中若有人行了耶和华所吩咐不可行的甚么事，误犯了罪，
chapters.en: And if any one of the common people sin through ignorance, while he doeth any of the commandments of the LORD which ought not to be done, and be guilty;

chapters.zh: 所犯的罪自己知道了，就要为所犯的罪牵一只没有残疾的母山羊为供物，
chapters.en: Or if his sin, which he hath sinned, come to his knowledge: then he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a female without blemish, for his sin which he hath sinned.

chapters.zh: 按手在赎罪祭牲的头上，在那宰燔祭牲的地方宰了。
chapters.en: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司要用指头蘸些羊的血，抹在燔祭坛的四角上，所有的血都要倒在坛的脚那里，
chapters.en: And the priest shall take of the blood thereof with his finger, and put upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar.

chapters.zh: 又要把羊所有的脂油都取下，正如取平安祭牲的脂油一样。祭司要在坛上焚烧，在耶和华面前作为馨香的祭，为他赎罪，他必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat is taken away from off the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the LORD; and the priest shall make an atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 人若牵一只绵羊羔为赎罪祭的供物，必要牵一只没有残疾的母羊，
chapters.en: And if he bring a lamb for a sin offering, he shall bring it a female without blemish.

chapters.zh: 按手在赎罪祭牲的头上，在那宰燔祭牲的地方宰了作赎罪祭。
chapters.en: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司要用指头蘸些赎罪祭牲的血，抹在燔祭坛的四角上，所有的血都要倒在坛的脚那里，
chapters.en: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar:

chapters.zh: 又要把所有的脂油都取下，正如取平安祭羊羔的脂油一样。祭司要按献给耶和华火祭的条例，烧在坛上。至于所犯的罪，祭司要为他赎了，他必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat of the lamb is taken away from the sacrifice of the peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them upon the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: and the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 若有人听见发誓的声音（或作：若有人听见叫人发誓的声音），他本是见证，却不把所看见的、所知道的说出来，这就是罪；他要担当他的罪孽。
chapters.en: And if a soul sin, and hear the voice of swearing, and a witness, whether he hath seen or known ; if he do not utter , then he shall bear his iniquity.

chapters.zh: 或是有人摸了不洁的物，无论是不洁的死兽，是不洁的死畜，是不洁的死虫，他却不知道，因此成了不洁，就有了罪。
chapters.en: Or if a soul touch any unclean thing, whether a carcase of an unclean beast, or a carcase of unclean cattle, or the carcase of unclean creeping things, and it be hidden from him; he also shall be unclean, and guilty.

chapters.zh: 或是他摸了别人的污秽，无论是染了甚么污秽，他却不知道，一知道了就有了罪。
chapters.en: Or if he touch the uncleanness of man, whatsoever uncleanness that a man shall be defiled withal, and it be hid from him; when he knoweth , then he shall be guilty.

chapters.zh: 或是有人嘴里冒失发誓，要行恶，要行善，无论人在甚么事上冒失发誓，他却不知道，一知道了就要在这其中的一件上有了罪。
chapters.en: Or if a soul swear, pronouncing with lips to do evil, or to do good, whatsoever that a man shall pronounce with an oath, and it be hid from him; when he knoweth , then he shall be guilty in one of these.

chapters.zh: 他有了罪的时候，就要承认所犯的罪，
chapters.en: And it shall be, when he shall be guilty in one of these , that he shall confess that he hath sinned in that :

chapters.zh: 并要因所犯的罪，把他的赎愆祭牲 ─ 就是羊群中的母羊，或是一只羊羔，或是一只山羊 ─ 牵到耶和华面前为赎罪祭。至于他的罪，祭司要为他赎了。
chapters.en: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD for his sin which he hath sinned, a female from the flock, a lamb or a kid of the goats, for a sin offering; and the priest shall make an atonement for him concerning his sin.

chapters.zh: 他的力量若不够献一只羊羔，就要因所犯的罪，把两只斑鸠或是两只雏鸽带到耶和华面前为赎愆祭：一只作赎罪祭，一只作燔祭。
chapters.en: And if he be not able to bring a lamb, then he shall bring for his trespass, which he hath committed, two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, unto the LORD; one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 把这些带到祭司那里，祭司就要先把那赎罪祭献上，从鸟的颈项上揪下头来，只是不可把鸟撕断，
chapters.en: And he shall bring them unto the priest, who shall offer which for the sin offering first, and wring off his head from his neck, but shall not divide asunder:

chapters.zh: 也把些赎罪祭牲的血弹在坛的旁边，剩下的血要流在坛的脚那里；这是赎罪祭。
chapters.en: And he shall sprinkle of the blood of the sin offering upon the side of the altar; and the rest of the blood shall be wrung out at the bottom of the altar: it a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 他要照例献第二只为燔祭。至于他所犯的罪，祭司要为他赎了，他必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall offer the second a burnt offering, according to the manner: and the priest shall make an atonement for him for his sin which he hath sinned, and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 他的力量若不够献两只斑鸠或是两只雏鸽，就要因所犯的罪带供物来，就是细面伊法十分之一为赎罪祭；不可加上油，也不可加上乳香，因为是赎罪祭。
chapters.en: But if he be not able to bring two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, then he that sinned shall bring for his offering the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour for a sin offering; he shall put no oil upon it, neither shall he put frankincense thereon: for it a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 他要把供物带到祭司那里，祭司要取出自己的一把来作为纪念，按献给耶和华火祭的条例烧在坛上；这是赎罪祭。
chapters.en: Then shall he bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take his handful of it, a memorial thereof, and burn on the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: it a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 至于他在这几件事中所犯的罪，祭司要为他赎了，他必蒙赦免。剩下的面都归与祭司，和素祭一样。
chapters.en: And the priest shall make an atonement for him as touching his sin that he hath sinned in one of these, and it shall be forgiven him: and shall be the priest's, as a meat offering.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 人若在耶和华的圣物上误犯了罪，有了过犯，就要照你所估的，按圣所的舍客勒拿银子，将赎愆祭牲 ─ 就是羊群中一只没有残疾的公绵羊 ─ 牵到耶和华面前为赎愆祭；
chapters.en: If a soul commit a trespass, and sin through ignorance, in the holy things of the LORD; then he shall bring for his trespass unto the LORD a ram without blemish out of the flocks, with thy estimation by shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for a trespass offering:

chapters.zh: 并且他因在圣物上的差错要偿还，另外加五分之一，都给祭司。祭司要用赎愆祭的公绵羊为他赎罪，他必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall make amends for the harm that he hath done in the holy thing, and shall add the fifth part thereto, and give it unto the priest: and the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering, and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 若有人犯罪，行了耶和华所吩咐不可行的甚么事，他虽然不知道，还是有了罪，就要担当他的罪孽；
chapters.en: And if a soul sin, and commit any of these things which are forbidden to be done by the commandments of the LORD; though he wist not, yet is he guilty, and shall bear his iniquity.

chapters.zh: 也要照你所估定的价，从羊群中牵一只没有残疾的公绵羊来，给祭司作赎愆祭。至于他误行的那错事，祭司要为他赎罪，他必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And he shall bring a ram without blemish out of the flock, with thy estimation, for a trespass offering, unto the priest: and the priest shall make an atonement for him concerning his ignorance wherein he erred and wist not, and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 这是赎愆祭，因他在耶和华面前实在有了罪。
chapters.en: It a trespass offering: he hath certainly trespassed against the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 若有人犯罪，干犯耶和华，在邻舍交付他的物上，或是在交易上行了诡诈，或是抢夺人的财物，或是欺压邻舍，
chapters.en: If a soul sin, and commit a trespass against the LORD, and lie unto his neighbour in that which was delivered him to keep, or in fellowship, or in a thing taken away by violence, or hath deceived his neighbour;

chapters.zh: 或是在捡了遗失的物上行了诡诈，说谎起誓，在这一切的事上犯了甚么罪；
chapters.en: Or have found that which was lost, and lieth concerning it, and sweareth falsely; in any of all these that a man doeth, sinning therein:

chapters.zh: 他既犯了罪，有了过犯，就要归还他所抢夺的，或是因欺压所得的，或是人交付他的，或是人遗失他所捡的物，
chapters.en: Then it shall be, because he hath sinned, and is guilty, that he shall restore that which he took violently away, or the thing which he hath deceitfully gotten, or that which was delivered him to keep, or the lost thing which he found,

chapters.zh: 或是他因甚么物起了假誓，就要如数归还，另外加上五分之一，在查出他有罪的日子要交还本主。
chapters.en: Or all that about which he hath sworn falsely; he shall even restore it in the principal, and shall add the fifth part more thereto, give it unto him to whom it appertaineth, in the day of his trespass offering.

chapters.zh: 也要照你所估定的价，把赎愆祭牲 ─ 就是羊群中一只没有残疾的公绵羊 ─ 牵到耶和华面前，给祭司为赎愆祭。
chapters.en: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD, a ram without blemish out of the flock, with thy estimation, for a trespass offering, unto the priest:

chapters.zh: 祭司要在耶和华面前为他赎罪；他无论行了甚么事，使他有了罪，都必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD: and it shall be forgiven him for any thing of all that he hath done in trespassing therein.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你要吩咐亚伦和他的子孙说，燔祭的条例乃是这样：燔祭要放在坛的柴上，从晚上到天亮，坛上的火要常常烧著。
chapters.en: Command Aaron and his sons, saying, This the law of the burnt offering: It the burnt offering, because of the burning upon the altar all night unto the morning, and the fire of the altar shall be burning in it.

chapters.zh: 祭司要穿上细麻布衣服，又要把细麻布裤子穿在身上，把坛上所烧的燔祭灰收起来，倒在坛的旁边；
chapters.en: And the priest shall put on his linen garment, and his linen breeches shall he put upon his flesh, and take up the ashes which the fire hath consumed with the burnt offering on the altar, and he shall put them beside the altar.

chapters.zh: 随后要脱去这衣服，穿上别的衣服，把灰拿到营外洁净之处。
chapters.en: And he shall put off his garments, and put on other garments, and carry forth the ashes without the camp unto a clean place.

chapters.zh: 坛上的火要在其上常常烧著，不可熄灭。祭司要每日早晨在上面烧柴，并要把燔祭摆在坛上，在其上烧平安祭牲的脂油。
chapters.en: And the fire upon the altar shall be burning in it; it shall not be put out: and the priest shall burn wood on it every morning, and lay the burnt offering in order upon it; and he shall burn thereon the fat of the peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 在坛上必有常常烧著的火，不可熄灭。
chapters.en: The fire shall ever be burning upon the altar; it shall never go out.

chapters.zh: 素祭的条例乃是这样：亚伦的子孙要在坛前把这祭献在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And this the law of the meat offering: the sons of Aaron shall offer it before the LORD, before the altar.

chapters.zh: 祭司要从其中 ─ 就是从素祭的细面中 ─ 取出自己的一把，又要取些油和素祭上所有的乳香，烧在坛上，奉给耶和华为馨香素祭的纪念。
chapters.en: And he shall take of it his handful, of the flour of the meat offering, and of the oil thereof, and all the frankincense which upon the meat offering, and shall burn upon the altar a sweet savour, the memorial of it, unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 所剩下的，亚伦和他子孙要吃，必在圣处不带酵而吃，要在会幕的院子里吃。
chapters.en: And the remainder thereof shall Aaron and his sons eat: with unleavened bread shall it be eaten in the holy place; in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation they shall eat it.

chapters.zh: 烤的时候不可搀酵。这是从所献给我的火祭中赐给他们的分，是至圣的，和赎罪祭并赎愆祭一样。
chapters.en: It shall not be baken with leaven. I have given it their portion of my offerings made by fire; it most holy, as the sin offering, and as the trespass offering.

chapters.zh: 凡献给耶和华的火祭，亚伦子孙中的男丁都要吃这一分，直到万代，作他们永得的分。摸这些祭物的，都要成为圣。
chapters.en: All the males among the children of Aaron shall eat of it. a statute for ever in your generations concerning the offerings of the LORD made by fire: every one that toucheth them shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 当亚伦受膏的日子，他和他子孙所要献给耶和华的供物，就是细面伊法十分之一，为常献的素祭：早晨一半，晚上一半。
chapters.en: This the offering of Aaron and of his sons, which they shall offer unto the LORD in the day when he is anointed; the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour for a meat offering perpetual, half of it in the morning, and half thereof at night.

chapters.zh: 要在铁鏊上用油调和做成，调匀了，你就拿进来；烤好了分成块子，献给耶和华为馨香的素祭。
chapters.en: In a pan it shall be made with oil; baken, thou shalt bring it in: the baken pieces of the meat offering shalt thou offer a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的子孙中，接续他为受膏的祭司，要把这素祭献上，要全烧给耶和华。这是永远的定例。
chapters.en: And the priest of his sons that is anointed in his stead shall offer it: a statute for ever unto the LORD; it shall be wholly burnt.

chapters.zh: 祭司的素祭都要烧了，却不可吃。
chapters.en: For every meat offering for the priest shall be wholly burnt: it shall not be eaten.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你对亚伦和他的子孙说，赎罪祭的条例乃是这样：要在耶和华面前、宰燔祭牲的地方宰赎罪祭牲；这是至圣的。
chapters.en: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, saying, This the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the LORD: it most holy.

chapters.zh: 为赎罪献这祭的祭司要吃，要在圣处，就是在会幕的院子里吃。
chapters.en: The priest that offereth it for sin shall eat it: in the holy place shall it be eaten, in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 凡摸这祭肉的要成为圣；这祭牲的血若弹在甚么衣服上，所弹的那一件要在圣处洗净。
chapters.en: Whatsoever shall touch the flesh thereof shall be holy: and when there is sprinkled of the blood thereof upon any garment, thou shalt wash that whereon it was sprinkled in the holy place.

chapters.zh: 惟有煮祭物的瓦器要打碎；若是煮在铜器里，这铜器要擦磨，在水中涮净。
chapters.en: But the earthen vessel wherein it is sodden shall be broken: and if it be sodden in a brasen pot, it shall be both scoured, and rinsed in water.

chapters.zh: 凡祭司中的男丁都可以吃；这是至圣的。
chapters.en: All the males among the priests shall eat thereof: it most holy.

chapters.zh: 凡赎罪祭，若将血带进会幕在圣所赎罪，那肉都不可吃，必用火焚烧。
chapters.en: And no sin offering, whereof of the blood is brought into the tabernacle of the congregation to reconcile in the holy , shall be eaten: it shall be burnt in the fire.

chapters.zh: 赎愆祭的条例乃是如此：这祭是至圣的。
chapters.en: Likewise this the law of the trespass offering: it most holy.

chapters.zh: 人在那里宰燔祭牲，也要在那里宰赎愆祭牲；其血，祭司要洒在坛的周围。
chapters.en: In the place where they kill the burnt offering shall they kill the trespass offering: and the blood thereof shall he sprinkle round about upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 又要将肥尾巴和盖脏的脂油，
chapters.en: And he shall offer of it all the fat thereof; the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards,

chapters.zh: 两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，就是靠腰两旁的脂油，并肝上的网子和腰子，一概取下。
chapters.en: And the two kidneys, and the fat that on them, which by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away:

chapters.zh: 祭司要在坛上焚烧，为献给耶和华的火祭，是赎愆祭。
chapters.en: And the priest shall burn them upon the altar an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it a trespass offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司中的男丁都可以吃这祭物；要在圣处吃，是至圣的。
chapters.en: Every male among the priests shall eat thereof: it shall be eaten in the holy place: it most holy.

chapters.zh: 赎罪祭怎样，赎愆祭也是怎样，两个祭是一个条例。献赎愆祭赎罪的祭司要得这祭物。
chapters.en: As the sin offering , so the trespass offering: one law for them: the priest that maketh atonement therewith shall have .

chapters.zh: 献燔祭的祭司，无论为谁奉献，要亲自得他所献那燔祭牲的皮。
chapters.en: And the priest that offereth any man's burnt offering, the priest shall have to himself the skin of the burnt offering which he hath offered.

chapters.zh: 凡在炉中烤的素祭和煎盘中做的，并铁鏊上做的，都要归那献祭的祭司。
chapters.en: And all the meat offering that is baken in the oven, and all that is dressed in the fryingpan, and in the pan, shall be the priest's that offereth it.

chapters.zh: 凡素祭，无论是油调和的是干的，都要归亚伦的子孙，大家均分。
chapters.en: And every meat offering, mingled with oil, and dry, shall all the sons of Aaron have, one as another.

chapters.zh: 人献与耶和华平安祭的条例乃是这样：
chapters.en: And this the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which he shall offer unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他若为感谢献上，就要用调油的无酵饼和抹油的无酵薄饼，并用油调匀细面做的饼，与感谢祭一同献上。
chapters.en: If he offer it for a thanksgiving, then he shall offer with the sacrifice of thanksgiving unleavened cakes mingled with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and cakes mingled with oil, of fine flour, fried.

chapters.zh: 要用有酵的饼和为感谢献的平安祭，与供物一同献上。
chapters.en: Besides the cakes, he shall offer his offering leavened bread with the sacrifice of thanksgiving of his peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 从各样的供物中，他要把一个饼献给耶和华为举祭，是要归给洒平安祭牲血的祭司。
chapters.en: And of it he shall offer one out of the whole oblation an heave offering unto the LORD, it shall be the priest's that sprinkleth the blood of the peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 为感谢献平安祭牲的肉，要在献的日子吃，一点不可留到早晨。
chapters.en: And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving shall be eaten the same day that it is offered; he shall not leave any of it until the morning.

chapters.zh: 若所献的是为还愿，或是甘心献的，必在献祭的日子吃，所剩下的第二天也可以吃。
chapters.en: But if the sacrifice of his offering a vow, or a voluntary offering, it shall be eaten the same day that he offereth his sacrifice: and on the morrow also the remainder of it shall be eaten:

chapters.zh: 但所剩下的祭肉，到第三天要用火焚烧；
chapters.en: But the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burnt with fire.

chapters.zh: 第三天若吃了平安祭的肉，这祭必不蒙悦纳，人所献的也不算为祭，反为可憎嫌的，吃这祭肉的，就必担当他的罪孽。
chapters.en: And if of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings be eaten at all on the third day, it shall not be accepted, neither shall it be imputed unto him that offereth it: it shall be an abomination, and the soul that eateth of it shall bear his iniquity.

chapters.zh: 挨了污秽物的肉就不可吃，要用火焚烧。至于平安祭的肉，凡洁净的人都要吃；
chapters.en: And the flesh that toucheth any unclean shall not be eaten; it shall be burnt with fire: and as for the flesh, all that be clean shall eat thereof.

chapters.zh: 只是献与耶和华平安祭的肉，人若不洁净而吃了，这人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: But the soul that eateth the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, that unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.

chapters.zh: 有人摸了甚么不洁净的物，或是人的不洁净，或是不洁净的牲畜，或是不洁可憎之物，吃了献与耶和华平安祭的肉，这人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Moreover the soul that shall touch any unclean the uncleanness of man, or unclean beast, or any abominable unclean , and eat of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which unto the LORD, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：牛的脂油、绵羊的脂油、山羊的脂油，你们都不可吃。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Ye shall eat no manner of fat, of ox, or of sheep, or of goat.

chapters.zh: 自死的和被野兽撕裂的，那脂油可以做别的使用，只是你们万不可吃。
chapters.en: And the fat of the beast that dieth of itself, and the fat of that which is torn with beasts, may be used in any other use: but ye shall in no wise eat of it.

chapters.zh: 无论何人吃了献给耶和华当火祭牲畜的脂油，那人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD, even the soul that eateth shall be cut off from his people.

chapters.zh: 在你们一切的住处，无论是雀鸟的血是野兽的血，你们都不可吃。
chapters.en: Moreover ye shall eat no manner of blood, of fowl or of beast, in any of your dwellings.

chapters.zh: 无论是谁吃血，那人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Whatsoever soul that eateth any manner of blood, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：献平安祭给耶和华的，要从平安祭中取些来奉给耶和华。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, He that offereth the sacrifice of his peace offerings unto the LORD shall bring his oblation unto the LORD of the sacrifice of his peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 他亲手献给耶和华的火祭，就是脂油和胸，要带来，好把胸在耶和华面前作摇祭，摇一摇。
chapters.en: His own hands shall bring the offerings of the LORD made by fire, the fat with the breast, it shall he bring, that the breast may be waved a wave offering before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 祭司要把脂油在坛上焚烧，但胸要归亚伦和他的子孙。
chapters.en: And the priest shall burn the fat upon the altar: but the breast shall be Aaron's and his sons'.

chapters.zh: 你们要从平安祭中把右腿作举祭，奉给祭司。
chapters.en: And the right shoulder shall ye give unto the priest an heave offering of the sacrifices of your peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 亚伦子孙中，献平安祭牲血和脂油的，要得这右腿为分；
chapters.en: He among the sons of Aaron, that offereth the blood of the peace offerings, and the fat, shall have the right shoulder for part.

chapters.zh: 因为我从以色列人的平安祭中，取了这摇的胸和举的腿给祭司亚伦和他子孙，作他们从以色列人中所永得的分。
chapters.en: For the wave breast and the heave shoulder have I taken of the children of Israel from off the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons by a statute for ever from among the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 这是从耶和华火祭中，作亚伦受膏的分和他子孙受膏的分，正在摩西（原文是他）叫他们前来给耶和华供祭司职分的日子，
chapters.en: This of the anointing of Aaron, and of the anointing of his sons, out of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, in the day he presented them to minister unto the LORD in the priest's office;

chapters.zh: 就是在摩西（原文是他）膏他们的日子，耶和华吩咐以色列人给他们的。这是他们世世代代永得的分。
chapters.en: Which the LORD commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them, a statute for ever throughout their generations.

chapters.zh: 这就是燔祭、素祭、赎罪祭、赎愆祭，和平安祭的条例，并承接圣职的礼，
chapters.en: This the law of the burnt offering, of the meat offering, and of the sin offering, and of the trespass offering, and of the consecrations, and of the sacrifice of the peace offerings;

chapters.zh: 都是耶和华在西乃山所吩咐摩西的，就是他在西乃旷野吩咐以色列人献供物给耶和华之日所说的。
chapters.en: Which the LORD commanded Moses in mount Sinai, in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their oblations unto the LORD, in the wilderness of Sinai.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你将亚伦和他儿子一同带来，并将圣衣、膏油，与赎罪祭的一只公牛、两只公绵羊、一筐无酵饼都带来，
chapters.en: Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil, and a bullock for the sin offering, and two rams, and a basket of unleavened bread;

chapters.zh: 又招聚会众到会幕门口。
chapters.en: And gather thou all the congregation together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 摩西就照耶和华所吩咐的行了；于是会众聚集在会幕门口。
chapters.en: And Moses did as the LORD commanded him; and the assembly was gathered together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 摩西告诉会众说：这就是耶和华所吩咐当行的事。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the congregation, This the thing which the LORD commanded to be done.

chapters.zh: 摩西带了亚伦和他儿子来，用水洗了他们。
chapters.en: And Moses brought Aaron and his sons, and washed them with water.

chapters.zh: 给亚伦穿上内袍，束上腰带，穿上外袍，又加上以弗得，用其上巧工织的带子把以弗得系在他身上，
chapters.en: And he put upon him the coat, and girded him with the girdle, and clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod upon him, and he girded him with the curious girdle of the ephod, and bound unto him therewith.

chapters.zh: 又给他戴上胸牌，把乌陵和土明放在胸牌内，
chapters.en: And he put the breastplate upon him: also he put in the breastplate the Urim and the Thummim.

chapters.zh: 把冠冕戴在他头上，在冠冕的前面钉上金牌，就是圣冠，都是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And he put the mitre upon his head; also upon the mitre, upon his forefront, did he put the golden plate, the holy crown; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 摩西用膏油抹帐幕和其中所有的，使他成圣；
chapters.en: And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the tabernacle and all that therein, and sanctified them.

chapters.zh: 又用膏油在坛上弹了七次，又抹了坛和坛的一切器皿，并洗濯盆和盆座，使他成圣；
chapters.en: And he sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all his vessels, both the laver and his foot, to sanctify them.

chapters.zh: 又把膏油倒在亚伦的头上膏他，使他成圣。
chapters.en: And he poured of the anointing oil upon Aaron's head, and anointed him, to sanctify him.

chapters.zh: 摩西带了亚伦的儿子来，给他们穿上内袍，束上腰带，包上裹头巾，都是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And Moses brought Aaron's sons, and put coats upon them, and girded them with girdles, and put bonnets upon them; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他牵了赎罪祭的公牛来，亚伦和他儿子按手在赎罪祭公牛的头上，
chapters.en: And he brought the bullock for the sin offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bullock for the sin offering.

chapters.zh: 就宰了公牛。摩西用指头蘸血，抹在坛上四角的周围，使坛洁净，把血倒在坛的脚那里，使坛成圣，坛就洁净了；
chapters.en: And he slew ; and Moses took the blood, and put upon the horns of the altar round about with his finger, and purified the altar, and poured the blood at the bottom of the altar, and sanctified it, to make reconciliation upon it.

chapters.zh: 又取脏上所有的脂油和肝上的网子，并两个腰子与腰子上的脂油，都烧在坛上；
chapters.en: And he took all the fat that upon the inwards, and the caul the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and Moses burned upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 惟有公牛，连皮带肉并粪，用火烧在营外，都是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: But the bullock, and his hide, his flesh, and his dung, he burnt with fire without the camp; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他奉上燔祭的公绵羊；亚伦和他儿子按手在羊的头上，
chapters.en: And he brought the ram for the burnt offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.

chapters.zh: 就宰了公羊。摩西把血洒在坛的周围，
chapters.en: And he killed ; and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.

chapters.zh: 把羊切成块子，把头和肉块并脂油都烧了。
chapters.en: And he cut the ram into pieces; and Moses burnt the head, and the pieces, and the fat.

chapters.zh: 用水洗了脏腑和腿，就把全羊烧在坛上为馨香的燔祭，是献给耶和华的火祭，都是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And he washed the inwards and the legs in water; and Moses burnt the whole ram upon the altar: it a burnt sacrifice for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他又奉上第二只公绵羊，就是承接圣职之礼的羊；亚伦和他儿子按手在羊的头上，
chapters.en: And he brought the other ram, the ram of consecration: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.

chapters.zh: 就宰了羊。摩西把些血抹在亚伦的右耳垂上和右手的大拇指上，并右脚的大拇指上，
chapters.en: And he slew ; and Moses took of the blood of it, and put upon the tip of Aaron's right ear, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.

chapters.zh: 又带了亚伦的儿子来，把些血抹在他们的右耳垂上和右手的大拇指上，并右脚的大拇指上，又把血洒在坛的周围。
chapters.en: And he brought Aaron's sons, and Moses put of the blood upon the tip of their right ear, and upon the thumbs of their right hands, and upon the great toes of their right feet: and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.

chapters.zh: 取脂油和肥尾巴，并脏上一切的脂油与肝上的网子，两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，并右腿，
chapters.en: And he took the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that upon the inwards, and the caul the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and the right shoulder:

chapters.zh: 再从耶和华面前、盛无酵饼的筐子里取出一个无酵饼，一个油饼，一个薄饼，都放在脂油和右腿上，
chapters.en: And out of the basket of unleavened bread, that before the LORD, he took one unleavened cake, and a cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and put on the fat, and upon the right shoulder:

chapters.zh: 把这一切放在亚伦的手上和他儿子的手上作摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇。
chapters.en: And he put all upon Aaron's hands, and upon his sons' hands, and waved them a wave offering before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 摩西从他们的手上拿下来，烧在坛上的燔祭上，都是为承接圣职献给耶和华馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And Moses took them from off their hands, and burnt on the altar upon the burnt offering: they consecrations for a sweet savour: it an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 摩西拿羊的胸作为摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇，是承接圣职之礼，归摩西的分，都是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And Moses took the breast, and waved it a wave offering before the LORD: of the ram of consecration it was Moses' part; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 摩西取点膏油和坛上的血，弹在亚伦和他的衣服上，并他儿子和他儿子的衣服上，使他和他们的衣服一同成圣。
chapters.en: And Moses took of the anointing oil, and of the blood which upon the altar, and sprinkled upon Aaron, upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon his sons' garments with him; and sanctified Aaron, his garments, and his sons, and his sons' garments with him.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦和他儿子说：把肉煮在会幕门口，在那里吃，又吃承接圣职筐子里的饼，按我所吩咐的说（或作：按所吩咐我的说）：这是亚伦和他儿子要吃的。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Aaron and to his sons, Boil the flesh the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and there eat it with the bread that in the basket of consecrations, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons shall eat it.

chapters.zh: 剩下的肉和饼，你们要用火焚烧。
chapters.en: And that which remaineth of the flesh and of the bread shall ye burn with fire.

chapters.zh: 你们七天不可出会幕的门，等到你们承接圣职的日子满了，因为主叫你们七天承接圣职。
chapters.en: And ye shall not go out of the door of the tabernacle of the congregation seven days, until the days of your consecration be at an end: for seven days shall he consecrate you.

chapters.zh: 像今天所行的都是耶和华吩咐行的，为你们赎罪。
chapters.en: As he hath done this day, the LORD hath commanded to do, to make an atonement for you.

chapters.zh: 七天你们要昼夜住在会幕门口，遵守耶和华的吩咐，免得你们死亡，因为所吩咐我的就是这样。
chapters.en: Therefore shall ye abide the door of the tabernacle of the congregation day and night seven days, and keep the charge of the LORD, that ye die not: for so I am commanded.

chapters.zh: 于是亚伦和他儿子行了耶和华藉著摩西所吩咐的一切事。
chapters.en: So Aaron and his sons did all things which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses.

chapters.zh: 到了第八天，摩西召了亚伦和他儿子，并以色列的众长老来，
chapters.en: And it came to pass on the eighth day, Moses called Aaron and his sons, and the elders of Israel;

chapters.zh: 对亚伦说：你当取牛群中的一只公牛犊作赎罪祭，一只公绵羊作燔祭，都要没有残疾的，献在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And he said unto Aaron, Take thee a young calf for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你也要对以色列人说：你们当取一只公山羊作赎罪祭，又取一只牛犊和一只绵羊羔，都要一岁、没有残疾的，作燔祭，
chapters.en: And unto the children of Israel thou shalt speak, saying, Take ye a kid of the goats for a sin offering; and a calf and a lamb, of the first year, without blemish, for a burnt offering;

chapters.zh: 又取一只公牛，一只公绵羊作平安祭，献在耶和华面前，并取调油的素祭，因为今天耶和华要向你们显现。
chapters.en: Also a bullock and a ram for peace offerings, to sacrifice before the LORD; and a meat offering mingled with oil: for to day the LORD will appear unto you.

chapters.zh: 于是他们把摩西所吩咐的，带到会幕前；全会众都近前来，站在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And they brought which Moses commanded before the tabernacle of the congregation: and all the congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：这是耶和华吩咐你们所当行的；耶和华的荣光就要向你们显现。
chapters.en: And Moses said, This the thing which the LORD commanded that ye should do: and the glory of the LORD shall appear unto you.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦说：你就近坛前，献你的赎罪祭和燔祭，为自己与百姓赎罪，又献上百姓的供物，为他们赎罪，都照耶和华所吩咐的。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Aaron, Go unto the altar, and offer thy sin offering, and thy burnt offering, and make an atonement for thyself, and for the people: and offer the offering of the people, and make an atonement for them; as the LORD commanded.

chapters.zh: 于是，亚伦就近坛前，宰了为自己作赎罪祭的牛犊。
chapters.en: Aaron therefore went unto the altar, and slew the calf of the sin offering, which for himself.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的儿子把血奉给他，他就把指头蘸在血中，抹在坛的四角上，又把血倒在坛脚那里。
chapters.en: And the sons of Aaron brought the blood unto him: and he dipped his finger in the blood, and put upon the horns of the altar, and poured out the blood at the bottom of the altar:

chapters.zh: 惟有赎罪祭的脂油和腰子，并肝上取的网子，都烧在坛上，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的；
chapters.en: But the fat, and the kidneys, and the caul above the liver of the sin offering, he burnt upon the altar; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 又用火将肉和皮烧在营外。
chapters.en: And the flesh and the hide he burnt with fire without the camp.

chapters.zh: 亚伦宰了燔祭牲，他儿子把血递给他，他就洒在坛的周围，
chapters.en: And he slew the burnt offering; and Aaron's sons presented unto him the blood, which he sprinkled round about upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 又把燔祭一块一块的、连头递给他，他都烧在坛上；
chapters.en: And they presented the burnt offering unto him, with the pieces thereof, and the head: and he burnt upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 又洗了脏腑和腿，烧在坛上的燔祭上。
chapters.en: And he did wash the inwards and the legs, and burnt upon the burnt offering on the altar.

chapters.zh: 他奉上百姓的供物，把那给百姓作赎罪祭的公山羊宰了，为罪献上，和先献的一样；
chapters.en: And he brought the people's offering, and took the goat, which the sin offering for the people, and slew it, and offered it for sin, as the first.

chapters.zh: 也奉上燔祭，照例而献。
chapters.en: And he brought the burnt offering, and offered it according to the manner.

chapters.zh: 他又奉上素祭，从其中取一满把，烧在坛上；这是在早晨的燔祭以外。
chapters.en: And he brought the meat offering, and took an handful thereof, and burnt upon the altar, beside the burnt sacrifice of the morning.

chapters.zh: 亚伦宰了那给百姓作平安祭的公牛和公绵羊。他儿子把血递给他，他就洒在坛的周围；
chapters.en: He slew also the bullock and the ram a sacrifice of peace offerings, which for the people: and Aaron's sons presented unto him the blood, which he sprinkled upon the altar round about,

chapters.zh: 又把公牛和公绵羊的脂油、肥尾巴，并盖脏的脂油与腰子，和肝上的网子，都递给他；
chapters.en: And the fat of the bullock and of the ram, the rump, and that which covereth , and the kidneys, and the caul the liver:

chapters.zh: 把脂油放在胸上，他就把脂油烧在坛上。
chapters.en: And they put the fat upon the breasts, and he burnt the fat upon the altar:

chapters.zh: 胸和右腿，亚伦当作摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇，都是照摩西所吩咐的。
chapters.en: And the breasts and the right shoulder Aaron waved a wave offering before the LORD; as Moses commanded.

chapters.zh: 亚伦向百姓举手，为他们祝福。他献了赎罪祭、燔祭、平安祭就下来了。
chapters.en: And Aaron lifted up his hand toward the people, and blessed them, and came down from offering of the sin offering, and the burnt offering, and peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦进入会幕，又出来为百姓祝福，耶和华的荣光就向众民显现。
chapters.en: And Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of the congregation, and came out, and blessed the people: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the people.

chapters.zh: 有火从耶和华面前出来，在坛上烧尽燔祭和脂油；众民一见，就都欢呼，俯伏在地。
chapters.en: And there came a fire out from before the LORD, and consumed upon the altar the burnt offering and the fat: when all the people saw, they shouted, and fell on their faces.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的儿子拿答、亚比户各拿自己的香炉，盛上火，加上香，在耶和华面前献上凡火，是耶和华没有吩咐他们的，
chapters.en: And Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, took either of them his censer, and put fire therein, and put incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the LORD, which he commanded them not.

chapters.zh: 就有火从耶和华面前出来，把他们烧灭，他们就死在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And there went out fire from the LORD, and devoured them, and they died before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 于是摩西对亚伦说：这就是耶和华所说：我在亲近我的人中要显为圣；在众民面前，我要得荣耀。亚伦就默默不言。
chapters.en: Then Moses said unto Aaron, This that the LORD spake, saying, I will be sanctified in them that come nigh me, and before all the people I will be glorified. And Aaron held his peace.

chapters.zh: 摩西召了亚伦叔父乌薛的儿子米沙利、以利撒反来，对他们说：上前来，把你们的亲属从圣所前抬到营外。
chapters.en: And Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said unto them, Come near, carry your brethren from before the sanctuary out of the camp.

chapters.zh: 于是二人上前来，把他们穿著袍子抬到营外，是照摩西所吩咐的。
chapters.en: So they went near, and carried them in their coats out of the camp; as Moses had said.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦和他儿子以利亚撒、以他玛说：不可蓬头散发，也不可撕裂衣裳，免得你们死亡，又免得耶和华向会众发怒；只要你们的弟兄以色列全家为耶和华所发的火哀哭。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons, Uncover not your heads, neither rend your clothes; lest ye die, and lest wrath come upon all the people: but let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which the LORD hath kindled.

chapters.zh: 你们也不可出会幕的门，恐怕你们死亡，因为耶和华的膏油在你们的身上。他们就照摩西的话行了。
chapters.en: And ye shall not go out from the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: for the anointing oil of the LORD upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 你和你儿子进会幕的时候，清酒、浓酒都不可喝，免得你们死亡；这要作你们世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: Do not drink wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: a statute for ever throughout your generations:

chapters.zh: 使你们可以将圣的、俗的，洁净的、不洁净的，分别出来；
chapters.en: And that ye may put difference between holy and unholy, and between unclean and clean;

chapters.zh: 又使你们可以将耶和华藉摩西晓谕以色列人的一切律例教训他们。
chapters.en: And that ye may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the LORD hath spoken unto them by the hand of Moses.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦和他剩下的儿子以利亚撒、以他玛说：你们献给耶和华火祭中所剩的素祭，要在坛旁不带酵而吃，因为是至圣的。
chapters.en: And Moses spake unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons that were left, Take the meat offering that remaineth of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, and eat it without leaven beside the altar: for it most holy:

chapters.zh: 你们要在圣处吃；因为在献给耶和华的火祭中，这是你的分和你儿子的分；所吩咐我的本是这样。
chapters.en: And ye shall eat it in the holy place, because it thy due, and thy sons' due, of the sacrifices of the LORD made by fire: for so I am commanded.

chapters.zh: 所摇的胸，所举的腿，你们要在洁净地方吃。你和你的儿女都要同吃；因为这些是从以色列人平安祭中给你，当你的分和你儿子的分。
chapters.en: And the wave breast and heave shoulder shall ye eat in a clean place; thou, and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee: for thy due, and thy sons' due, are given out of the sacrifices of peace offerings of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 所举的腿，所摇的胸，他们要与火祭的脂油一同带来当摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇；这要归你和你儿子，当作永得的分，都是照耶和华所吩咐的。
chapters.en: The heave shoulder and the wave breast shall they bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave a wave offering before the LORD; and it shall be thine, and thy sons' with thee, by a statute for ever; as the LORD hath commanded.

chapters.zh: 当下摩西急切的寻找作赎罪祭的公山羊，谁知已经焚烧了，便向亚伦剩下的儿子以利亚撒、以他玛发怒，说：
chapters.en: And Moses diligently sought the goat of the sin offering, and, behold, it was burnt: and he was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, the sons of Aaron left , saying,

chapters.zh: 这赎罪祭既是至圣的，主又给了你们，为要你们担当会众的罪孽，在耶和华面前为他们赎罪，你们为何没有在圣所吃呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin offering in the holy place, seeing it most holy, and hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?

chapters.zh: 看哪，这祭牲的血并没有拿到圣所里去，你们本当照我所吩咐的，在圣所里吃这祭肉。
chapters.en: Behold, the blood of it was not brought in within the holy : ye should indeed have eaten it in the holy , as I commanded.

chapters.zh: 亚伦对摩西说：今天他们在耶和华面前献上赎罪祭和燔祭，我又遇见这样的灾，若今天吃了赎罪祭，耶和华岂能看为美呢？
chapters.en: And Aaron said unto Moses, Behold, this day have they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD; and such things have befallen me: and I had eaten the sin offering to day, should it have been accepted in the sight of the LORD?

chapters.zh: 摩西听见这话，便以为美。
chapters.en: And when Moses heard , he was content.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying unto them,

chapters.zh: 你们晓谕以色列人说，在地上一切走兽中可吃的乃是这些：
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, These the beasts which ye shall eat among all the beasts that on the earth.

chapters.zh: 凡蹄分两瓣、倒嚼的走兽，你们都可以吃。
chapters.en: Whatsoever parteth the hoof, and is clovenfooted, cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that shall ye eat.

chapters.zh: 但那倒嚼或分蹄之中不可吃的乃是：骆驼 ─ 因为倒嚼不分蹄，就与你们不洁净；
chapters.en: Nevertheless these shall ye not eat of them that chew the cud, or of them that divide the hoof: the camel, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he unclean unto you.

chapters.zh: 沙番 ─ 因为倒嚼不分蹄，就与你们不洁净；
chapters.en: And the coney, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he unclean unto you.

chapters.zh: 兔子 ─ 因为倒嚼不分蹄，就与你们不洁净；
chapters.en: And the hare, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he unclean unto you.

chapters.zh: 猪 ─ 因为蹄分两瓣，却不倒嚼，就与你们不洁净。
chapters.en: And the swine, though he divide the hoof, and be clovenfooted, yet he cheweth not the cud; he unclean to you.

chapters.zh: 这些兽的肉，你们不可吃；死的，你们不可摸，都与你们不洁净。
chapters.en: Of their flesh shall ye not eat, and their carcase shall ye not touch; they unclean to you.

chapters.zh: 水中可吃的乃是这些：凡在水里、海里、河里、有翅有鳞的，都可以吃。
chapters.en: These shall ye eat of all that in the waters: whatsoever hath fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, them shall ye eat.

chapters.zh: 凡在海里、河里，并一切水里游动的活物，无翅无鳞的，你们都当以为可憎。
chapters.en: And all that have not fins and scales in the seas, and in the rivers, of all that move in the waters, and of any living thing which in the waters, they an abomination unto you:

chapters.zh: 这些无翅无鳞、以为可憎的，你们不可吃他的肉；死的也当以为可憎。
chapters.en: They shall be even an abomination unto you; ye shall not eat of their flesh, but ye shall have their carcases in abomination.

chapters.zh: 凡水里无翅无鳞的，你们都当以为可憎。
chapters.en: Whatsoever hath no fins nor scales in the waters, that an abomination unto you.

chapters.zh: 雀鸟中你们当以为可憎、不可吃的乃是：雕、狗头雕、红头雕、
chapters.en: And these ye shall have in abomination among the fowls; they shall not be eaten, they an abomination: the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the ospray,

chapters.zh: 鹞鹰、小鹰与其类；
chapters.en: And the vulture, and the kite after his kind;

chapters.zh: 乌鸦与其类；
chapters.en: Every raven after his kind;

chapters.zh: 鸵鸟、夜鹰、鱼鹰、鹰与其类；
chapters.en: And the owl, and the night hawk, and the cuckow, and the hawk after his kind,

chapters.zh: 鸮鸟、鸬鹚、猫头鹰、
chapters.en: And the little owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl,

chapters.zh: 角鸱、鹈鹕、秃雕、
chapters.en: And the swan, and the pelican, and the gier eagle,

chapters.zh: 鹳、鹭鸶与其类；戴鵀与蝙蝠。
chapters.en: And the stork, the heron after her kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.

chapters.zh: 凡有翅膀用四足爬行的物，你们都当以为可憎。
chapters.en: All fowls that creep, going upon four, an abomination unto you.

chapters.zh: 只是有翅膀用四足爬行的物中，有足有腿，在地上蹦跳的，你们还可以吃。
chapters.en: Yet these may ye eat of every flying creeping thing that goeth upon four, which have legs above their feet, to leap withal upon the earth;

chapters.zh: 其中有蝗虫、蚂蚱、蟋蟀与其类；蚱蜢与其类；这些你们都可以吃。
chapters.en: these of them ye may eat; the locust after his kind, and the bald locust after his kind, and the beetle after his kind, and the grasshopper after his kind.

chapters.zh: 但是有翅膀有四足的爬物，你们都当以为可憎。
chapters.en: But all flying creeping things, which have four feet, an abomination unto you.

chapters.zh: 这些都能使你们不洁净。凡摸了死的，必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: And for these ye shall be unclean: whosoever toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 凡拿了死的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服。
chapters.en: And whosoever beareth of the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 凡走兽分蹄不成两瓣、也不倒嚼的，是与你们不洁净；凡摸了的就不洁净。
chapters.en: of every beast which divideth the hoof, and not clovenfooted, nor cheweth the cud, unclean unto you: every one that toucheth them shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 凡四足的走兽，用掌行走的，是与你们不洁净；摸其尸的，必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: And whatsoever goeth upon his paws, among all manner of beasts that go on four, those unclean unto you: whoso toucheth their carcase shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 拿其尸的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服。这些是与你们不洁净的。
chapters.en: And he that beareth the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: they unclean unto you.

chapters.zh: 地上爬物与你们不洁净的乃是这些：鼬鼠、鼫鼠、蜥蜴与其类；
chapters.en: These also unclean unto you among the creeping things that creep upon the earth; the weasel, and the mouse, and the tortoise after his kind,

chapters.zh: 壁虎、龙子、守宫、蛇医、蝘蜓。
chapters.en: And the ferret, and the chameleon, and the lizard, and the snail, and the mole.

chapters.zh: 这些爬物都是与你们不洁净的。在他死了以后，凡摸了的，必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: These unclean to you among all that creep: whosoever doth touch them, when they be dead, shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 其中死了的，掉在甚么东西上，这东西就不洁净，无论是木器、衣服、皮子、口袋，不拘是做甚么工用的器皿，须要放在水中，必不洁净到晚上，到晚上才洁净了。
chapters.en: And upon whatsoever of them, when they are dead, doth fall, it shall be unclean; whether any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skin, or sack, whatsoever vessel , wherein work is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the even; so it shall be cleansed.

chapters.zh: 若有死了掉在瓦器里的，其中不拘有甚么，就不洁净，你们要把这瓦器打破了。
chapters.en: And every earthen vessel, whereinto of them falleth, whatsoever in it shall be unclean; and ye shall break it.

chapters.zh: 其中一切可吃的食物，沾水的就不洁净，并且那样器皿中一切可喝的，也必不洁净。
chapters.en: Of all meat which may be eaten, on which water cometh shall be unclean: and all drink that may be drunk in every vessel shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 其中已死的，若有一点掉在甚么物件上，那物件就不洁净，不拘是炉子，是锅台，就要打碎，都不洁净，也必与你们不洁净。
chapters.en: And every whereupon of their carcase falleth shall be unclean; oven, or ranges for pots, they shall be broken down: they unclean, and shall be unclean unto you.

chapters.zh: 但是泉源或是聚水的池子仍是洁净；惟挨了那死的，就不洁净。
chapters.en: Nevertheless a fountain or pit, plenty of water, shall be clean: but that which toucheth their carcase shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 若是死的，有一点掉在要种的子粒上，子粒仍是洁净；
chapters.en: And if of their carcase fall upon any sowing seed which is to be sown, it clean.

chapters.zh: 若水已经浇在子粒上，那死的有一点掉在上头，这子粒就与你们不洁净。
chapters.en: But if water be put upon the seed, and of their carcase fall thereon, it unclean unto you.

chapters.zh: 你们可吃的走兽若是死了，有人摸他，必不洁净到晚上；
chapters.en: And if any beast, of which ye may eat, die; he that toucheth the carcase thereof shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 有人吃那死了的走兽，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服；拿了死走兽的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服。
chapters.en: And he that eateth of the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: he also that beareth the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 凡地上的爬物是可憎的，都不可吃。
chapters.en: And every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth an abomination; it shall not be eaten.

chapters.zh: 凡用肚子行走的和用四足行走的，或是有许多足的，就是一切爬在地上的，你们都不可吃，因为是可憎的。
chapters.en: Whatsoever goeth upon the belly, and whatsoever goeth upon four, or whatsoever hath more feet among all creeping things that creep upon the earth, them ye shall not eat; for they an abomination.

chapters.zh: 你们不可因甚么爬物使自己成为可憎的，也不可因这些使自己不洁净，以致染了污秽。
chapters.en: Ye shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creepeth, neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should be defiled thereby.

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神；所以你们要成为圣洁，因为我是圣洁的。你们也不可在地上的爬物污秽自己。
chapters.en: For I the LORD your God: ye shall therefore sanctify yourselves, and ye shall be holy; for I holy: neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

chapters.zh: 我是把你们从埃及地领出来的耶和华，要作你们的神；所以你们要圣洁，因为我是圣洁的。
chapters.en: For I the LORD that bringeth you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: ye shall therefore be holy, for I holy.

chapters.zh: 这是走兽、飞鸟，和水中游动的活物，并地上爬物的条例。
chapters.en: This the law of the beasts, and of the fowl, and of every living creature that moveth in the waters, and of every creature that creepeth upon the earth:

chapters.zh: 要把洁净的和不洁净的，可吃的与不可吃的活物，都分别出来。
chapters.en: To make a difference between the unclean and the clean, and between the beast that may be eaten and the beast that may not be eaten.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：若有妇人怀孕生男孩，他就不洁净七天，像在月经污秽的日子不洁净一样。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a woman have conceived seed, and born a man child: then she shall be unclean seven days; according to the days of the separation for her infirmity shall she be unclean.

chapters.zh: 第八天，要给婴孩行割礼。
chapters.en: And in the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.

chapters.zh: 妇人在产血不洁之中，要家居三十三天。他洁净的日子未满，不可摸圣物，也不可进入圣所。
chapters.en: And she shall then continue in the blood of her purifying three and thirty days; she shall touch no hallowed thing, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying be fulfilled.

chapters.zh: 他若生女孩，就不洁净两个七天，像污秽的时候一样，要在产血不洁之中，家居六十六天。
chapters.en: But if she bear a maid child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her separation: and she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and six days.

chapters.zh: 满了洁净的日子，无论是为男孩是为女孩，他要把一岁的羊羔为燔祭，一只雏鸽或是一只斑鸠为赎罪祭，带到会幕门口交给祭司。
chapters.en: And when the days of her purifying are fulfilled, for a son, or for a daughter, she shall bring a lamb of the first year for a burnt offering, and a young pigeon, or a turtledove, for a sin offering, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest:

chapters.zh: 祭司要献在耶和华面前，为他赎罪，他的血源就洁净了。这条例是为生育的妇人，无论是生男生女。
chapters.en: Who shall offer it before the LORD, and make an atonement for her; and she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This the law for her that hath born a male or a female.

chapters.zh: 他的力量不够献一只羊羔，他就要取两只斑鸠或是两只雏鸽，一只为燔祭，一只为赎罪祭。祭司要为他赎罪，他就洁净了。
chapters.en: And if she be not able to bring a lamb, then she shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons; the one for the burnt offering, and the other for a sin offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for her, and she shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 人的肉皮上若长了疖子，或长了癣，或长了火斑，在他肉皮上成了大痲疯的灾病，就要将他带到祭司亚伦或亚伦作祭司的一个子孙面前。
chapters.en: When a man shall have in the skin of his flesh a rising, a scab, or bright spot, and it be in the skin of his flesh the plague of leprosy; then he shall be brought unto Aaron the priest, or unto one of his sons the priests:

chapters.zh: 祭司要察看肉皮上的灾病，若灾病处的毛已经变白，灾病的现象深于肉上的皮，这便是大痲疯的灾病。祭司要察看他，定他为不洁净。
chapters.en: And the priest shall look on the plague in the skin of the flesh: and the hair in the plague is turned white, and the plague in sight deeper than the skin of his flesh, it a plague of leprosy: and the priest shall look on him, and pronounce him unclean.

chapters.zh: 若火斑在他肉皮上是白的，现象不深于皮，其上的毛也没有变白，祭司就要将有灾病的人关锁七天。
chapters.en: If the bright spot white in the skin of his flesh, and in sight not deeper than the skin, and the hair thereof be not turned white; then the priest shall shut up the plague seven days:

chapters.zh: 第七天，祭司要察看他，若看灾病止住了，没有在皮上发散，祭司还要将他关锁七天。
chapters.en: And the priest shall look on him the seventh day: and, behold, the plague in his sight be at a stay, the plague spread not in the skin; then the priest shall shut him up seven days more:

chapters.zh: 第七天，祭司要再察看他，若灾病发暗，而且没有在皮上发散，祭司要定他为洁净，原来是癣；那人就要洗衣服，得为洁净。
chapters.en: And the priest shall look on him again the seventh day: and, behold, the plague somewhat dark, the plague spread not in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him clean: it a scab: and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.

chapters.zh: 但他为得洁净，将身体给祭司察看以后，癣若在皮上发散开了，他要再将身体给祭司察看。
chapters.en: But if the scab spread much abroad in the skin, after that he hath been seen of the priest for his cleansing, he shall be seen of the priest again:

chapters.zh: 祭司要察看，癣若在皮上发散，就要定他为不洁净，是大痲疯。
chapters.en: And the priest see that, behold, the scab spreadeth in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it a leprosy.

chapters.zh: 人有了大痲疯的灾病，就要将他带到祭司面前。
chapters.en: When the plague of leprosy is in a man, then he shall be brought unto the priest;

chapters.zh: 祭司要察看，皮上若长了白疖，使毛变白，在长白疖之处有了红瘀肉，
chapters.en: And the priest shall see : and, behold, the rising white in the skin, and it have turned the hair white, and quick raw flesh in the rising;

chapters.zh: 这是肉皮上的旧大痲疯，祭司要定他为不洁净，不用将他关锁，因为他是不洁净了。
chapters.en: It an old leprosy in the skin of his flesh, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean, and shall not shut him up: for he unclean.

chapters.zh: 大痲疯若在皮上四外发散，长满了患灾病人的皮，据祭司察看，从头到脚无处不有，
chapters.en: And if a leprosy break out abroad in the skin, and the leprosy cover all the skin of the plague from his head even to his foot, wheresoever the priest looketh;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看，全身的肉若长满了大痲疯，就要定那患灾病的为洁净；全身都变为白，他乃洁净了。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall consider: and, behold, the leprosy have covered all his flesh, he shall pronounce clean the plague: it is all turned white: he clean.

chapters.zh: 但红肉几时显在他的身上就几时不洁净。
chapters.en: But when raw flesh appeareth in him, he shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 祭司一看那红肉就定他为不洁净。红肉本是不洁净，是大痲疯。
chapters.en: And the priest shall see the raw flesh, and pronounce him to be unclean: the raw flesh unclean: it a leprosy.

chapters.zh: 红肉若复原，又变白了，他就要来见祭司。
chapters.en: Or if the raw flesh turn again, and be changed unto white, he shall come unto the priest;

chapters.zh: 祭司要察看，灾病处若变白了，祭司就要定那患灾病的为洁净，他乃洁净了。
chapters.en: And the priest shall see him: and, behold, the plague be turned into white; then the priest shall pronounce clean the plague: he clean.

chapters.zh: 人若在皮肉上长疮，却治好了，
chapters.en: The flesh also, in which, in the skin thereof, was a boil, and is healed,

chapters.zh: 在长疮之处又起了白疖，或是白中带红的火斑，就要给祭司察看。
chapters.en: And in the place of the boil there be a white rising, or a bright spot, white, and somewhat reddish, and it be shewed to the priest;

chapters.zh: 祭司要察看，若现象洼于皮，其上的毛也变白了，就要定他为不洁净，是大痲疯的灾病发在疮中。
chapters.en: And if, when the priest seeth it, behold, it in sight lower than the skin, and the hair thereof be turned white; the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it a plague of leprosy broken out of the boil.

chapters.zh: 祭司若察看，其上没有白毛，也没有洼于皮，乃是发暗，就要将他关锁七天。
chapters.en: But if the priest look on it, and, behold, no white hairs therein, and it not lower than the skin, but somewhat dark; then the priest shall shut him up seven days:

chapters.zh: 若在皮上发散开了，祭司就要定他为不洁净，是灾病。
chapters.en: And if it spread much abroad in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it a plague.

chapters.zh: 火斑若在原处止住，没有发散，便是疮的痕迹，祭司就要定他为洁净。
chapters.en: But if the bright spot stay in his place, spread not, it a burning boil; and the priest shall pronounce him clean.

chapters.zh: 人的皮肉上若起了火毒，火毒的瘀肉成了火斑，或是白中带红的，或是全白的，
chapters.en: Or if there be flesh, in the skin whereof a hot burning, and the quick that burneth have a white bright spot, somewhat reddish, or white;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看，火斑中的毛若变白了，现象又深于皮，是大痲疯在火毒中发出，就要定他为不洁净，是大痲疯的灾病。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall look upon it: and, behold, the hair in the bright spot be turned white, and it sight deeper than the skin; it a leprosy broken out of the burning: wherefore the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it the plague of leprosy.

chapters.zh: 但是祭司察看，在火斑中若没有白毛，也没有洼于皮，乃是发暗，就要将他关锁七天。
chapters.en: But if the priest look on it, and, behold, no white hair in the bright spot, and it no lower than the skin, but somewhat dark; then the priest shall shut him up seven days:

chapters.zh: 到第七天，祭司要察看他，火斑若在皮上发散开了，就要定他为不洁净，是大痲疯的灾病。
chapters.en: And the priest shall look upon him the seventh day: if it be spread much abroad in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it the plague of leprosy.

chapters.zh: 火斑若在原处止住，没有在皮上发散，乃是发暗，是起的火毒，祭司要定他为洁净，不过是火毒的痕迹。
chapters.en: And if the bright spot stay in his place, spread not in the skin, but it somewhat dark; it a rising of the burning, and the priest shall pronounce him clean: for it an inflammation of the burning.

chapters.zh: 无论男女，若在头上有灾病，或是男人胡须上有灾病，
chapters.en: If a man or woman have a plague upon the head or the beard;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看；这灾病现象若深于皮，其间有细黄毛，就要定他为不洁净，这是头疥，是头上或是胡须上的大痲疯。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall see the plague: and, behold, if it in sight deeper than the skin; in it a yellow thin hair; then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it a dry scall, a leprosy upon the head or beard.

chapters.zh: 祭司若察看头疥的灾病，现象不深于皮，其间也没有黑毛，就要将长头疥灾病的关锁七天。
chapters.en: And if the priest look on the plague of the scall, and, behold, it not in sight deeper than the skin, and no black hair in it; then the priest shall shut up the plague of the scall seven days:

chapters.zh: 第七天，祭司要察看灾病，若头疥没有发散，其间也没有黄毛，头疥的现象不深于皮，
chapters.en: And in the seventh day the priest shall look on the plague: and, behold, the scall spread not, and there be in it no yellow hair, and the scall not in sight deeper than the skin;

chapters.zh: 那人就要剃去须发，但他不可剃头疥之处。祭司要将那长头疥的，再关锁七天。
chapters.en: He shall be shaven, but the scall shall he not shave; and the priest shall shut up the scall seven days more:

chapters.zh: 第七天，祭司要察看头疥，头疥若没有在皮上发散，现象也不深于皮，就要定他为洁净，他要洗衣服，便成为洁净。
chapters.en: And in the seventh day the priest shall look on the scall: and, behold, the scall be not spread in the skin, nor in sight deeper than the skin; then the priest shall pronounce him clean: and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.

chapters.zh: 但他得洁净以后，头疥若在皮上发散开了，
chapters.en: But if the scall spread much in the skin after his cleansing;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看他。头疥若在皮上发散，就不必找那黄毛，他是不洁净了。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall look on him: and, behold, if the scall be spread in the skin, the priest shall not seek for yellow hair; he unclean.

chapters.zh: 祭司若看头疥已经止住，其间也长了黑毛，头疥已然痊愈，那人是洁净了，就要定他为洁净。
chapters.en: But if the scall be in his sight at a stay, and there is black hair grown up therein; the scall is healed, he clean: and the priest shall pronounce him clean.

chapters.zh: 无论男女，皮肉上若起了火斑，就是白火斑，
chapters.en: If a man also or a woman have in the skin of their flesh bright spots, white bright spots;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看，他们肉皮上的火斑若白中带黑，这是皮上发出的白癣，那人是洁净了。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall look: and, behold, the bright spots in the skin of their flesh darkish white; it a freckled spot groweth in the skin; he clean.

chapters.zh: 人头上的发若掉了，他不过是头秃，还是洁净。
chapters.en: And the man whose hair is fallen off his head, he bald; he clean.

chapters.zh: 他顶前若掉了头发，他不过是顶门秃，还是洁净。
chapters.en: And he that hath his hair fallen off from the part of his head toward his face, he forehead bald: he clean.

chapters.zh: 头秃处或是顶门秃处若有白中带红的灾病，这就是大痲疯发在他头秃处或是顶门秃处，
chapters.en: And if there be in the bald head, or bald forehead, a white reddish sore; it a leprosy sprung up in his bald head, or his bald forehead.

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看，他起的那灾病若在头秃处或是顶门秃处有白中带红的，像肉皮上大痲疯的现象，
chapters.en: Then the priest shall look upon it: and, behold, the rising of the sore white reddish in his bald head, or in his bald forehead, as the leprosy appeareth in the skin of the flesh;

chapters.zh: 那人就是长大痲疯，不洁净的，祭司总要定他为不洁净，他的灾病是在头上。
chapters.en: He is a leprous man, he unclean: the priest shall pronounce him utterly unclean; his plague in his head.

chapters.zh: 身上有长大痲疯灾病的，他的衣服要撕裂，也要蓬头散发，蒙著上唇，喊叫说：不洁净了！不洁净了！
chapters.en: And the leper in whom the plague , his clothes shall be rent, and his head bare, and he shall put a covering upon his upper lip, and shall cry, Unclean, unclean.

chapters.zh: 灾病在他身上的日子，他便是不洁净；他既是不洁净，就要独居营外。
chapters.en: All the days wherein the plague in him he shall be defiled; he unclean: he shall dwell alone; without the camp his habitation .

chapters.zh: 染了大痲疯灾病的衣服，无论是羊毛衣服、是麻布衣服，
chapters.en: The garment also that the plague of leprosy is in, a woollen garment, or a linen garment;

chapters.zh: 无论是在经上、在纬上，是麻布的、是羊毛的，是在皮子上，或在皮子做的甚么物件上，
chapters.en: Whether in the warp, or woof; of linen, or of woollen; whether in a skin, or in any thing made of skin;

chapters.zh: 或在衣服上、皮子上，经上、纬上，或在皮子做的甚么物件上，这灾病若是发绿，或是发红，是大痲疯的灾病，要给祭司察看。
chapters.en: And if the plague be greenish or reddish in the garment, or in the skin, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin; it a plague of leprosy, and shall be shewed unto the priest:

chapters.zh: 祭司就要察看那灾病，把染了灾病的物件关锁七天。
chapters.en: And the priest shall look upon the plague, and shut up the plague seven days:

chapters.zh: 第七天，他要察看那灾病，灾病或在衣服上，经上、纬上，皮子上，若发散，这皮子无论当作何用，这灾病是蚕食的大痲疯，都是不洁净了。
chapters.en: And he shall look on the plague on the seventh day: if the plague be spread in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in a skin, in any work that is made of skin; the plague a fretting leprosy; it unclean.

chapters.zh: 那染了灾病的衣服，或是经上、纬上，羊毛上，麻衣上，或是皮子做的甚么物件上，他都要焚烧；因为这是蚕食的大痲疯，必在火中焚烧。
chapters.en: He shall therefore burn that garment, whether warp or woof, in woollen or in linen, or any thing of skin, wherein the plague is: for it a fretting leprosy; it shall be burnt in the fire.

chapters.zh: 祭司要察看，若灾病在衣服上，经上、纬上，或是皮子做的甚么物件上，没有发散，
chapters.en: And if the priest shall look, and, behold, the plague be not spread in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要吩咐他们，把染了灾病的物件洗了，再关锁七天。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall command that they wash wherein the plague , and he shall shut it up seven days more:

chapters.zh: 洗过以后，祭司要察看，那物件若没有变色，灾病也没有消散，那物件就不洁净，是透重的灾病，无论正面反面，都要在火中焚烧。
chapters.en: And the priest shall look on the plague, after that it is washed: and, behold, the plague have not changed his colour, and the plague be not spread; it unclean; thou shalt burn it in the fire; it fret inward, it bare within or without.

chapters.zh: 洗过以后，祭司要察看，若见那灾病发暗，他就要把那灾病从衣服上、皮子上、经上、纬上，都撕去。
chapters.en: And if the priest look, and, behold, the plague somewhat dark after the washing of it; then he shall rend it out of the garment, or out of the skin, or out of the warp, or out of the woof:

chapters.zh: 若仍现在衣服上，或是经上、纬上、皮子做的甚么物件上，这就是灾病又发了、必用火焚烧那染灾病的物件。
chapters.en: And if it appear still in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin; it a spreading : thou shalt burn that wherein the plague with fire.

chapters.zh: 所洗的衣服，或是经，或是纬，或是皮子做的甚么物件，若灾病离开了，要再洗，就洁净了。
chapters.en: And the garment, either warp, or woof, or whatsoever thing of skin , which thou shalt wash, if the plague be departed from them, then it shall be washed the second time, and shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 这就是大痲疯灾病的条例，无论是在羊毛衣服上，麻布衣服上，经上、纬上，和皮子做的甚么物件上，可以定为洁净或是不洁净。
chapters.en: This the law of the plague of leprosy in a garment of woollen or linen, either in the warp, or woof, or any thing of skins, to pronounce it clean, or to pronounce it unclean.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 长大痲疯得洁净的日子，其例乃是这样：要带他去见祭司；
chapters.en: This shall be the law of the leper in the day of his cleansing: He shall be brought unto the priest:

chapters.zh: 祭司要出到营外察看，若见他的大痲疯痊愈了
chapters.en: And the priest shall go forth out of the camp; and the priest shall look, and, behold, the plague of leprosy be healed in the leper;

chapters.zh: 就要吩咐人为那求洁净的拿两只洁净的活鸟和香柏木、朱红色线，并牛膝草来。
chapters.en: Then shall the priest command to take for him that is to be cleansed two birds alive clean, and cedar wood, and scarlet, and hyssop:

chapters.zh: 祭司要吩咐用瓦器盛活水，把一只鸟宰在上面。
chapters.en: And the priest shall command that one of the birds be killed in an earthen vessel over running water:

chapters.zh: 至于那只活鸟，祭司要把他和香柏木、朱红色线并牛膝草一同蘸于宰在活水上的鸟血中，
chapters.en: As for the living bird, he shall take it, and the cedar wood, and the scarlet, and the hyssop, and shall dip them and the living bird in the blood of the bird killed over the running water:

chapters.zh: 用以在那长大痲疯求洁净的人身上洒七次，就定他为洁净，又把活鸟放在田野里。
chapters.en: And he shall sprinkle upon him that is to be cleansed from the leprosy seven times, and shall pronounce him clean, and shall let the living bird loose into the open field.

chapters.zh: 求洁净的人当洗衣服，剃去毛发，用水洗澡，就洁净了；然后可以进营，只是要在自己的帐棚外居住七天。
chapters.en: And he that is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes, and shave off all his hair, and wash himself in water, that he may be clean: and after that he shall come into the camp, and shall tarry abroad out of his tent seven days.

chapters.zh: 第七天，再把头上所有的头发与胡须、眉毛，并全身的毛，都剃了；又要洗衣服，用水洗身，就洁净了。
chapters.en: But it shall be on the seventh day, that he shall shave all his hair off his head and his beard and his eyebrows, even all his hair he shall shave off: and he shall wash his clothes, also he shall wash his flesh in water, and he shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 第八天，他要取两只没有残疾的公羊羔和一只没有残疾、一岁的母羊羔，又要把调油的细面伊法十分之三为素祭，并油一罗革，一同取来。
chapters.en: And on the eighth day he shall take two he lambs without blemish, and one ewe lamb of the first year without blemish, and three tenth deals of fine flour a meat offering, mingled with oil, and one log of oil.

chapters.zh: 行洁净之礼的祭司要将那求洁净的人和这些东西安置在会幕门口、耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And the priest that maketh clean shall present the man that is to be made clean, and those things, before the LORD, the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:

chapters.zh: 祭司要取一只公羊羔献为赎愆祭，和那一罗革油一同作摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇；
chapters.en: And the priest shall take one he lamb, and offer him for a trespass offering, and the log of oil, and wave them a wave offering before the LORD:

chapters.zh: 把公羊羔宰于圣地，就是宰赎罪祭牲和燔祭牲之地。赎愆祭要归祭司，与赎罪祭一样，是至圣的。
chapters.en: And he shall slay the lamb in the place where he shall kill the sin offering and the burnt offering, in the holy place: for as the sin offering the priest's, the trespass offering: it most holy:

chapters.zh: 祭司要取些赎愆祭牲的血，抹在求洁净人的右耳垂上和右手的大拇指上，并右脚的大拇指上。
chapters.en: And the priest shall take of the blood of the trespass offering, and the priest shall put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot:

chapters.zh: 祭司要从那一罗革油中取些倒在自己的左手掌里，
chapters.en: And the priest shall take of the log of oil, and pour into the palm of his own left hand:
