chapters.zh: 把右手的一个指头蘸在左手的油里，在耶和华面前用指头弹七次。
chapters.en: And the priest shall dip his right finger in the oil that in his left hand, and shall sprinkle of the oil with his finger seven times before the LORD:

chapters.zh: 将手里所剩的油抹在那求洁净人的右耳垂上和右手的大拇指上，并右脚的大拇指上，就是抹在赎愆祭牲的血上。
chapters.en: And of the rest of the oil that in his hand shall the priest put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot, upon the blood of the trespass offering:

chapters.zh: 祭司手里所剩的油要抹在那求洁净人的头上，在耶和华面前为他赎罪。
chapters.en: And the remnant of the oil that in the priest's hand he shall pour upon the head of him that is to be cleansed: and the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 祭司要献赎罪祭，为那本不洁净、求洁净的人赎罪；然后要宰燔祭牲，
chapters.en: And the priest shall offer the sin offering, and make an atonement for him that is to be cleansed from his uncleanness; and afterward he shall kill the burnt offering:

chapters.zh: 把燔祭和素祭献在坛上，为他赎罪，他就洁净了。
chapters.en: And the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the meat offering upon the altar: and the priest shall make an atonement for him, and he shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 他若贫穷不能预备够数，就要取一只公羊羔作赎愆祭，可以摇一摇，为他赎罪；也要把调油的细面伊法十分之一为素祭，和油一罗革一同取来；
chapters.en: And if he poor, and cannot get so much; then he shall take one lamb a trespass offering to be waved, to make an atonement for him, and one tenth deal of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering, and a log of oil;

chapters.zh: 又照他的力量取两只斑鸠或是两只雏鸽，一只作赎罪祭，一只作燔祭。
chapters.en: And two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, such as he is able to get; and the one shall be a sin offering, and the other a burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 第八天，要为洁净，把这些带到会幕门口、耶和华面前，交给祭司。
chapters.en: And he shall bring them on the eighth day for his cleansing unto the priest, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 祭司要把赎愆祭的羊羔和那一罗革油一同作摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇。
chapters.en: And the priest shall take the lamb of the trespass offering, and the log of oil, and the priest shall wave them a wave offering before the LORD:

chapters.zh: 要宰了赎愆祭的羊羔，取些赎愆祭牲的血，抹在那求洁净人的右耳垂上和右手的大拇指上，并右脚的大拇指上。
chapters.en: And he shall kill the lamb of the trespass offering, and the priest shall take of the blood of the trespass offering, and put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot:

chapters.zh: 祭司要把些油倒在自己的左手掌里，
chapters.en: And the priest shall pour of the oil into the palm of his own left hand:

chapters.zh: 把左手里的油，在耶和华面前，用右手的一个指头弹七次，
chapters.en: And the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger of the oil that in his left hand seven times before the LORD:

chapters.zh: 又把手里的油抹些在那求洁净人的右耳垂上和右手的大拇指上，并右脚的大拇指上，就是抹赎愆祭之血的原处。
chapters.en: And the priest shall put of the oil that in his hand upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot, upon the place of the blood of the trespass offering:

chapters.zh: 祭司手里所剩的油要抹在那求洁净人的头上，在耶和华面前为他赎罪。
chapters.en: And the rest of the oil that in the priest's hand he shall put upon the head of him that is to be cleansed, to make an atonement for him before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 那人又要照他的力量献上一只斑鸠或是一只雏鸽，
chapters.en: And he shall offer the one of the turtledoves, or of the young pigeons, such as he can get;

chapters.zh: 就是他所能办的，一只为赎罪祭，一只为燔祭，与素祭一同献上；祭司要在耶和华面前为他赎罪。
chapters.en: such as he is able to get, the one a sin offering, and the other a burnt offering, with the meat offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for him that is to be cleansed before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 这是那有大痲疯灾病的人、不能将关乎得洁净之物预备够数的条例。
chapters.en: This the law in whom the plague of leprosy, whose hand is not able to get to his cleansing.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们到了我赐给你们为业的迦南地，我若使你们所得为业之地的房屋中有大痲疯的灾病，
chapters.en: When ye be come into the land of Canaan, which I give to you for a possession, and I put the plague of leprosy in a house of the land of your possession;

chapters.zh: 房主就要去告诉祭司说：据我看，房屋中似乎有灾病。
chapters.en: And he that owneth the house shall come and tell the priest, saying, It seemeth to me as it were a plague in the house:

chapters.zh: 祭司还没有进去察看灾病以前，就要吩咐人把房子腾空，免得房子里所有的都成了不洁净；然后祭司要进去察看房子。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall command that they empty the house, before the priest go to see the plague, that all that in the house be not made unclean: and afterward the priest shall go in to see the house:

chapters.zh: 他要察看那灾病，灾病若在房子的墙上有发绿或发红的凹斑纹，现象洼于墙，
chapters.en: And he shall look on the plague, and, behold, the plague in the walls of the house with hollow strakes, greenish or reddish, which in sight lower than the wall;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要出到房门外，把房子封锁七天。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall go out of the house to the door of the house, and shut up the house seven days:

chapters.zh: 第七天，祭司要再去察看，灾病若在房子的墙上发散，
chapters.en: And the priest shall come again the seventh day, and shall look: and, behold, the plague be spread in the walls of the house;

chapters.zh: 就要吩咐人把那有灾病的石头挖出来，扔在城外不洁净之处；
chapters.en: Then the priest shall command that they take away the stones in which the plague , and they shall cast them into an unclean place without the city:

chapters.zh: 也要叫人刮房内的四围，所刮掉的灰泥要倒在城外不洁净之处；
chapters.en: And he shall cause the house to be scraped within round about, and they shall pour out the dust that they scrape off without the city into an unclean place:

chapters.zh: 又要用别的石头代替那挖出来的石头，要另用灰泥墁房子。
chapters.en: And they shall take other stones, and put in the place of those stones; and he shall take other morter, and shall plaister the house.

chapters.zh: 他挖出石头，刮了房子，墁了以后，灾病若在房子里又发现，
chapters.en: And if the plague come again, and break out in the house, after that he hath taken away the stones, and after he hath scraped the house, and after it is plaistered;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要进去察看，灾病若在房子里发散，这就是房内蚕食的大痲疯，是不洁净。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall come and look, and, behold, the plague be spread in the house, it a fretting leprosy in the house: it unclean.

chapters.zh: 他就要拆毁房子，把石头、木头、灰泥都搬到城外不洁净之处。
chapters.en: And he shall break down the house, the stones of it, and the timber thereof, and all the morter of the house; and he shall carry forth out of the city into an unclean place.

chapters.zh: 在房子封锁的时候，进去的人必不洁净到晚上；
chapters.en: Moreover he that goeth into the house all the while that it is shut up shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 在房子里躺著的必洗衣服；在房子里吃饭的也必洗衣服。
chapters.en: And he that lieth in the house shall wash his clothes; and he that eateth in the house shall wash his clothes.

chapters.zh: 房子墁了以后，祭司若进去察看，见灾病在房内没有发散，就要定房子为洁净，因为灾病已经消除。
chapters.en: And if the priest shall come in, and look , and, behold, the plague hath not spread in the house, after the house was plaistered: then the priest shall pronounce the house clean, because the plague is healed.

chapters.zh: 要为洁净房子取两只鸟和香柏木、朱红色线并牛膝草，
chapters.en: And he shall take to cleanse the house two birds, and cedar wood, and scarlet, and hyssop:

chapters.zh: 用瓦器盛活水，把一只鸟宰在上面，
chapters.en: And he shall kill the one of the birds in an earthen vessel over running water:

chapters.zh: 把香柏木、牛膝草、朱红色线，并那活鸟，都蘸在被宰的鸟血中与活水中，用以洒房子七次。
chapters.en: And he shall take the cedar wood, and the hyssop, and the scarlet, and the living bird, and dip them in the blood of the slain bird, and in the running water, and sprinkle the house seven times:

chapters.zh: 要用鸟血、活水、活鸟、香柏木、牛膝草，并朱红色线，洁净那房子。
chapters.en: And he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird, and with the running water, and with the living bird, and with the cedar wood, and with the hyssop, and with the scarlet:

chapters.zh: 但要把活鸟放在城外田野里。这样洁净房子（原文是为房子赎罪），房子就洁净了。
chapters.en: But he shall let go the living bird out of the city into the open fields, and make an atonement for the house: and it shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 这是为各类大痲疯的灾病和头疥，
chapters.en: This the law for all manner of plague of leprosy, and scall,

chapters.zh: 并衣服与房子的大痲疯，
chapters.en: And for the leprosy of a garment, and of a house,

chapters.zh: 以及疖子、癣、火斑所立的条例，
chapters.en: And for a rising, and for a scab, and for a bright spot:

chapters.zh: 指明何时为洁净，何时为不洁净。这是大痲疯的条例。
chapters.en: To teach when unclean, and when clean: this the law of leprosy.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们晓谕以色列人说：人若身患漏症，他因这漏症就不洁净了。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When any man hath a running issue out of his flesh, his issue he unclean.

chapters.zh: 他患漏症，无论是下流的，是止住的，都是不洁净。
chapters.en: And this shall be his uncleanness in his issue: whether his flesh run with his issue, or his flesh be stopped from his issue, it his uncleanness.

chapters.zh: 他所躺的床都为不洁净，所坐的物也为不洁净。
chapters.en: Every bed, whereon he lieth that hath the issue, is unclean: and every thing, whereon he sitteth, shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 凡摸那床的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And whosoever toucheth his bed shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 那坐患漏症人所坐之物的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And he that sitteth on thing whereon he sat that hath the issue shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 那摸患漏症人身体的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And he that toucheth the flesh of him that hath the issue shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 若患漏症人吐在洁净的人身上，那人必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And if he that hath the issue spit upon him that is clean; then he shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 患漏症人所骑的鞍子也为不洁净。
chapters.en: And what saddle soever he rideth upon that hath the issue shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 凡摸了他身下之物的，必不洁净到晚上；拿了那物的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And whosoever toucheth any thing that was under him shall be unclean until the even: and he that beareth those things shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 患漏症的人没有用水涮手，无论摸了谁，谁必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And whomsoever he toucheth that hath the issue, and hath not rinsed his hands in water, he shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 患漏症人所摸的瓦器就必打破；所摸的一切木器也必用水涮洗。
chapters.en: And the vessel of earth, that he toucheth which hath the issue, shall be broken: and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed in water.

chapters.zh: 患漏症的人痊愈了，就要为洁净自己计算七天，也必洗衣服，用活水洗身，就洁净了。
chapters.en: And when he that hath an issue is cleansed of his issue; then he shall number to himself seven days for his cleansing, and wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in running water, and shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 第八天，要取两只斑鸠或是两只雏鸽，来到会幕门口、耶和华面前，把鸟交给祭司。
chapters.en: And on the eighth day he shall take to him two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, and come before the LORD unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and give them unto the priest:

chapters.zh: 祭司要献上一只为赎罪祭，一只为燔祭；因那人患的漏症，祭司要在耶和华面前为他赎罪。
chapters.en: And the priest shall offer them, the one a sin offering, and the other a burnt offering; and the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD for his issue.

chapters.zh: 人若梦遗，他必不洁净到晚上，并要用水洗全身。
chapters.en: And if any man's seed of copulation go out from him, then he shall wash all his flesh in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 无论是衣服是皮子，被精所染，必不洁净到晚上，并要用水洗。
chapters.en: And every garment, and every skin, whereon is the seed of copulation, shall be washed with water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 若男女交合，两个人必不洁净到晚上，并要用水洗澡。
chapters.en: The woman also with whom man shall lie seed of copulation, they shall bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 女人行经，必污秽七天；凡摸他的，必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: And if a woman have an issue, her issue in her flesh be blood, she shall be put apart seven days: and whosoever toucheth her shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 女人在污秽之中，凡他所躺的物件都为不洁净，所坐的物件也都不洁净。
chapters.en: And every thing that she lieth upon in her separation shall be unclean: every thing also that she sitteth upon shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 凡摸他床的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And whosoever toucheth her bed shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 凡摸他所坐甚么物件的，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And whosoever toucheth any thing that she sat upon shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 在女人的床上，或在他坐的物上，若有别的物件，人一摸了，必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: And if it on bed, or on any thing whereon she sitteth, when he toucheth it, he shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 男人若与那女人同房，染了他的污秽，就要七天不洁净；所躺的床也为不洁净。
chapters.en: And if any man lie with her at all, and her flowers be upon him, he shall be unclean seven days; and all the bed whereon he lieth shall be unclean.

chapters.zh: 女人若在经期以外患多日的血漏，或是经期过长，有了漏症，他就因这漏症不洁净，与他在经期不洁净一样。
chapters.en: And if a woman have an issue of her blood many days out of the time of her separation, or if it run beyond the time of her separation; all the days of the issue of her uncleanness shall be as the days of her separation: she unclean.

chapters.zh: 他在患漏症的日子所躺的床、所坐的物都要看为不洁净，与他月经的时候一样。
chapters.en: Every bed whereon she lieth all the days of her issue shall be unto her as the bed of her separation: and whatsoever she sitteth upon shall be unclean, as the uncleanness of her separation.

chapters.zh: 凡摸这些物件的，就为不洁净，必不洁净到晚上，并要洗衣服，用水洗澡。
chapters.en: And whosoever toucheth those things shall be unclean, and shall wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 女人的漏症若好了，就要计算七天，然后才为洁净。
chapters.en: But if she be cleansed of her issue, then she shall number to herself seven days, and after that she shall be clean.

chapters.zh: 第八天，要取两只斑鸠或是两只雏鸽，带到会幕门口给祭司。
chapters.en: And on the eighth day she shall take unto her two turtles, or two young pigeons, and bring them unto the priest, to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 祭司要献一只为赎罪祭，一只为燔祭；因那人血漏不洁，祭司要在耶和华面前为他赎罪。
chapters.en: And the priest shall offer the one a sin offering, and the other a burnt offering; and the priest shall make an atonement for her before the LORD for the issue of her uncleanness.

chapters.zh: 你们要这样使以色列人与他们的污秽隔绝，免得他们玷污我的帐幕，就因自己的污秽死亡。
chapters.en: Thus shall ye separate the children of Israel from their uncleanness; that they die not in their uncleanness, when they defile my tabernacle that among them.

chapters.zh: 这是患漏症和梦遗而不洁净的，
chapters.en: This the law of him that hath an issue, and whose seed goeth from him, and is defiled therewith;

chapters.zh: 并有月经病的和患漏症的，无论男女，并人与不洁净女人同房的条例。
chapters.en: And of her that is sick of her flowers, and of him that hath an issue, of the man, and of the woman, and of him that lieth with her that is unclean.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的两个儿子近到耶和华面前死了。死了之后，耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron, when they offered before the LORD, and died;

chapters.zh: 要告诉你哥哥亚伦，不可随时进圣所的幔子内、到柜上的施恩座前，免得他死亡，因为我要从云中显现在施恩座上。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Speak unto Aaron thy brother, that he come not at all times into the holy within the vail before the mercy seat, which upon the ark; that he die not: for I will appear in the cloud upon the mercy seat.

chapters.zh: 亚伦进圣所，要带一只公牛犊为赎罪祭，一只公绵羊为燔祭。
chapters.en: Thus shall Aaron come into the holy : with a young bullock for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 要穿上细麻布圣内袍，把细麻布裤子穿在身上，腰束细麻布带子，头戴细麻布冠冕；这都是圣服。他要用水洗身，然后穿戴。
chapters.en: He shall put on the holy linen coat, and he shall have the linen breeches upon his flesh, and shall be girded with a linen girdle, and with the linen mitre shall he be attired: these holy garments; therefore shall he wash his flesh in water, and put them on.

chapters.zh: 要从以色列会众取两只公山羊为赎罪祭，一只公绵羊为燔祭。
chapters.en: And he shall take of the congregation of the children of Israel two kids of the goats for a sin offering, and one ram for a burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 亚伦要把赎罪祭的公牛奉上，为自己和本家赎罪；
chapters.en: And Aaron shall offer his bullock of the sin offering, which for himself, and make an atonement for himself, and for his house.

chapters.zh: 也要把两只公山羊安置在会幕门口、耶和华面前，
chapters.en: And he shall take the two goats, and present them before the LORD the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 为那两只羊拈阄，一阄归与耶和华，一阄归与阿撒泻勒。
chapters.en: And Aaron shall cast lots upon the two goats; one lot for the LORD, and the other lot for the scapegoat.

chapters.zh: 亚伦要把那拈阄归与耶和华的羊献为赎罪祭，
chapters.en: And Aaron shall bring the goat upon which the LORD'S lot fell, and offer him a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 但那拈阄归与阿撒泻勒的羊要活著安置在耶和华面前，用以赎罪，打发人送到旷野去，归与阿撒泻勒。
chapters.en: But the goat, on which the lot fell to be the scapegoat, shall be presented alive before the LORD, to make an atonement with him, to let him go for a scapegoat into the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 亚伦要把赎罪祭的公牛带来宰了，为自己和本家赎罪；
chapters.en: And Aaron shall bring the bullock of the sin offering, which for himself, and shall make an atonement for himself, and for his house, and shall kill the bullock of the sin offering which for himself:

chapters.zh: 拿香炉，从耶和华面前的坛上盛满火炭，又拿一捧捣细的香料，都带入幔子内，
chapters.en: And he shall take a censer full of burning coals of fire from off the altar before the LORD, and his hands full of sweet incense beaten small, and bring within the vail:

chapters.zh: 在耶和华面前，把香放在火上，使香的烟云遮掩法柜上的施恩座，免得他死亡；
chapters.en: And he shall put the incense upon the fire before the LORD, that the cloud of the incense may cover the mercy seat that upon the testimony, that he die not:

chapters.zh: 也要取些公牛的血，用指头弹在施恩座的东面，又在施恩座的前面弹血七次。
chapters.en: And he shall take of the blood of the bullock, and sprinkle with his finger upon the mercy seat eastward; and before the mercy seat shall he sprinkle of the blood with his finger seven times.

chapters.zh: 随后他要宰那为百姓作赎罪祭的公山羊，把羊的血带入幔子内，弹在施恩座的上面和前面，好像弹公牛的血一样。
chapters.en: Then shall he kill the goat of the sin offering, that for the people, and bring his blood within the vail, and do with that blood as he did with the blood of the bullock, and sprinkle it upon the mercy seat, and before the mercy seat:

chapters.zh: 他因以色列人诸般的污秽、过犯，就是他们一切的罪愆，当这样在圣所行赎罪之礼，并因会幕在他们污秽之中，也要照样而行。
chapters.en: And he shall make an atonement for the holy , because of the uncleanness of the children of Israel, and because of their transgressions in all their sins: and so shall he do for the tabernacle of the congregation, that remaineth among them in the midst of their uncleanness.

chapters.zh: 他进圣所赎罪的时候，会幕里不可有人，直等到他为自己和本家并以色列全会众赎了罪出来。
chapters.en: And there shall be no man in the tabernacle of the congregation when he goeth in to make an atonement in the holy , until he come out, and have made an atonement for himself, and for his household, and for all the congregation of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他出来，要到耶和华面前的坛那里，在坛上行赎罪之礼，又要取些公牛的血和公山羊的血，抹在坛上四角的周围；
chapters.en: And he shall go out unto the altar that before the LORD, and make an atonement for it; and shall take of the blood of the bullock, and of the blood of the goat, and put upon the horns of the altar round about.

chapters.zh: 也要用指头把血弹在坛上七次，洁净了坛，从坛上除掉以色列人诸般的污秽，使坛成圣。
chapters.en: And he shall sprinkle of the blood upon it with his finger seven times, and cleanse it, and hallow it from the uncleanness of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 亚伦为圣所和会幕并坛献完了赎罪祭，就要把那只活著的公山羊奉上。
chapters.en: And when he hath made an end of reconciling the holy , and the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar, he shall bring the live goat:

chapters.zh: 两手按在羊头上，承认以色列人诸般的罪孽过犯，就是他们一切的罪愆，把这罪都归在羊的头上，藉著所派之人的手，送到旷野去。
chapters.en: And Aaron shall lay both his hands upon the head of the live goat, and confess over him all the iniquities of the children of Israel, and all their transgressions in all their sins, putting them upon the head of the goat, and shall send away by the hand of a fit man into the wilderness:

chapters.zh: 要把这羊放在旷野，这羊要担当他们一切的罪孽，带到无人之地。
chapters.en: And the goat shall bear upon him all their iniquities unto a land not inhabited: and he shall let go the goat in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 亚伦要进会幕，把他进圣所时所穿的细麻布衣服脱下，放在那里，
chapters.en: And Aaron shall come into the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall put off the linen garments, which he put on when he went into the holy , and shall leave them there:

chapters.zh: 又要在圣处用水洗身，穿上衣服，出来，把自己的燔祭和百姓的燔祭献上，为自己和百姓赎罪。
chapters.en: And he shall wash his flesh with water in the holy place, and put on his garments, and come forth, and offer his burnt offering, and the burnt offering of the people, and make an atonement for himself, and for the people.

chapters.zh: 赎罪祭牲的脂油要在坛上焚烧。
chapters.en: And the fat of the sin offering shall he burn upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 那放羊归与阿撒泻勒的人要洗衣服，用水洗身，然后进营。
chapters.en: And he that let go the goat for the scapegoat shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward come into the camp.

chapters.zh: 作赎罪祭的公牛和公山羊的血既带入圣所赎罪，这牛羊就要搬到营外，将皮、肉、粪用火焚烧。
chapters.en: And the bullock the sin offering, and the goat the sin offering, whose blood was brought in to make atonement in the holy , shall carry forth without the camp; and they shall burn in the fire their skins, and their flesh, and their dung.

chapters.zh: 焚烧的人要洗衣服，用水洗身，然后进营。
chapters.en: And he that burneth them shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp.

chapters.zh: 每逢七月初十日，你们要刻苦己心，无论是本地人，是寄居在你们中间的外人，甚么工都不可做；这要作你们永远的定例。
chapters.en: And shall be a statute for ever unto you: in the seventh month, on the tenth of the month, ye shall afflict your souls, and do no work at all, one of your own country, or a stranger that sojourneth among you:

chapters.zh: 因在这日要为你们赎罪，使你们洁净。你们要在耶和华面前得以洁净，脱尽一切的罪愆。
chapters.en: For on that day shall make an atonement for you, to cleanse you, ye may be clean from all your sins before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 这日你们要守为圣安息日，要刻苦己心；这为永远的定例。
chapters.en: It a sabbath of rest unto you, and ye shall afflict your souls, by a statute for ever.

chapters.zh: 那受膏、接续他父亲承接圣职的祭司要穿上细麻布的圣衣，行赎罪之礼。
chapters.en: And the priest, whom he shall anoint, and whom he shall consecrate to minister in the priest's office in his father's stead, shall make the atonement, and shall put on the linen clothes, the holy garments:

chapters.zh: 他要在至圣所和会幕与坛行赎罪之礼，并要为众祭司和会众的百姓赎罪。
chapters.en: And he shall make an atonement for the holy sanctuary, and he shall make an atonement for the tabernacle of the congregation, and for the altar, and he shall make an atonement for the priests, and for all the people of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 这要作你们永远的定例 ─ 就是因以色列人一切的罪，要一年一次为他们赎罪。于是，亚伦照耶和华所吩咐摩西的行了。
chapters.en: And this shall be an everlasting statute unto you, to make an atonement for the children of Israel for all their sins once a year. And he did as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕亚伦和他儿子并以色列众人说，耶和华所吩咐的乃是这样：
chapters.en: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them; This the thing which the LORD hath commanded, saying,

chapters.zh: 凡以色列家中的人宰公牛，或是绵羊羔，或是山羊，不拘宰于营内营外，
chapters.en: What man soever of the house of Israel, that killeth an ox, or lamb, or goat, in the camp, or that killeth out of the camp,

chapters.zh: 若未曾牵到会幕门口、耶和华的帐幕前献给耶和华为供物，流血的罪必归到那人身上。他流了血，要从民中剪除。
chapters.en: And bringeth it not unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, to offer an offering unto the LORD before the tabernacle of the LORD; blood shall be imputed unto that man; he hath shed blood; and that man shall be cut off from among his people:

chapters.zh: 这是为要使以色列人把他们在田野里所献的祭带到会幕门口、耶和华面前，交给祭司，献与耶和华为平安祭。
chapters.en: To the end that the children of Israel may bring their sacrifices, which they offer in the open field, even that they may bring them unto the LORD, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest, and offer them peace offerings unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 祭司要把血洒在会幕门口、耶和华的坛上，把脂油焚烧，献给耶和华为馨香的祭。
chapters.en: And the priest shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar of the LORD the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and burn the fat for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他们不可再献祭给他们行邪淫所随从的鬼魔（原文作公山羊）；这要作他们世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: And they shall no more offer their sacrifices unto devils, after whom they have gone a whoring. This shall be a statute for ever unto them throughout their generations.

chapters.zh: 你要晓谕他们说：凡以色列家中的人，或是寄居在他们中间的外人，献燔祭或是平安祭，
chapters.en: And thou shalt say unto them, Whatsoever man of the house of Israel, or of the strangers which sojourn among you, that offereth a burnt offering or sacrifice,

chapters.zh: 若不带到会幕门口献给耶和华，那人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: And bringeth it not unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, to offer it unto the LORD; even that man shall be cut off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 凡以色列家中的人，或是寄居在他们中间的外人，若吃甚么血，我必向那吃血的人变脸，把他从民中剪除。
chapters.en: And whatsoever man of the house of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn among you, that eateth any manner of blood; I will even set my face against that soul that eateth blood, and will cut him off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 因为活物的生命是在血中。我把这血赐给你们，可以在坛上为你们的生命赎罪；因血里有生命，所以能赎罪。
chapters.en: For the life of the flesh in the blood: and I have given it to you upon the altar to make an atonement for your souls: for it the blood maketh an atonement for the soul.

chapters.zh: 因此，我对以色列人说：你们都不可吃血；寄居在你们中间的外人也不可吃血。
chapters.en: Therefore I said unto the children of Israel, No soul of you shall eat blood, neither shall any stranger that sojourneth among you eat blood.

chapters.zh: 凡以色列人，或是寄居在他们中间的外人，若打猎得了可吃的禽兽，必放出他的血来，用土掩盖。
chapters.en: And whatsoever man of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn among you, which hunteth and catcheth any beast or fowl that may be eaten; he shall even pour out the blood thereof, and cover it with dust.

chapters.zh: 论到一切活物的生命，就在血中。所以我对以色列人说：无论甚么活物的血，你们都不可吃，因为一切活物的血就是他的生命。凡吃了血的，必被剪除。
chapters.en: For the life of all flesh; the blood of it for the life thereof: therefore I said unto the children of Israel, Ye shall eat the blood of no manner of flesh: for the life of all flesh the blood thereof: whosoever eateth it shall be cut off.

chapters.zh: 凡吃自死的，或是被野兽撕裂的，无论是本地人，是寄居的，必不洁净到晚上，都要洗衣服，用水洗身，到了晚上才为洁净。
chapters.en: And every soul that eateth that which died , or that which was torn one of your own country, or a stranger, he shall both wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the even: then shall he be clean.

chapters.zh: 但他若不洗衣服，也不洗身，就必担当他的罪孽。
chapters.en: But if he wash not, nor bathe his flesh; then he shall bear his iniquity.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, I am the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们从前住的埃及地，那里人的行为，你们不可效法，我要领你们到的迦南地，那里人的行为也不可效法，也不可照他们的恶俗行。
chapters.en: After the doings of the land of Egypt, wherein ye dwelt, shall ye not do: and after the doings of the land of Canaan, whither I bring you, shall ye not do: neither shall ye walk in their ordinances.

chapters.zh: 你们要遵我的典章，守我的律例，按此而行。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Ye shall do my judgments, and keep mine ordinances, to walk therein: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 所以，你们要守我的律例典章；人若遵行，就必因此活著。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Ye shall therefore keep my statutes, and my judgments: which if a man do, he shall live in them: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们都不可露骨肉之亲的下体，亲近他们。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: None of you shall approach to any that is near of kin to him, to uncover nakedness: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可露你母亲的下体，羞辱了你父亲。他是你的母亲，不可露他的下体。
chapters.en: The nakedness of thy father, or the nakedness of thy mother, shalt thou not uncover: she thy mother; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.

chapters.zh: 不可露你继母的下体；这本是你父亲的下体。
chapters.en: The nakedness of thy father's wife shalt thou not uncover: it thy father's nakedness.

chapters.zh: 你的姊妹，不拘是异母同父的，是异父同母的，无论是生在家生在外的，都不可露他们的下体。
chapters.en: The nakedness of thy sister, the daughter of thy father, or daughter of thy mother, born at home, or born abroad, their nakedness thou shalt not uncover.

chapters.zh: 不可露你孙女或是外孙女的下体，露了他们的下体就是露了自己的下体。
chapters.en: The nakedness of thy son's daughter, or of thy daughter's daughter, their nakedness thou shalt not uncover: for theirs thine own nakedness.

chapters.zh: 你继母从你父亲生的女儿本是你的妹妹，不可露他的下体。
chapters.en: The nakedness of thy father's wife's daughter, begotten of thy father, she thy sister, thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.

chapters.zh: 不可露你姑母的下体；他是你父亲的骨肉之亲。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father's sister: she thy father's near kinswoman.

chapters.zh: 不可露你姨母的下体；他是你母亲的骨肉之亲。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother's sister: for she thy mother's near kinswoman.

chapters.zh: 不可亲近你伯叔之妻，羞辱了你伯叔；他是你的伯叔母。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father's brother, thou shalt not approach to his wife: she thine aunt.

chapters.zh: 不可露你儿妇的下体；他是你儿子的妻，不可露他的下体。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy daughter in law: she thy son's wife; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.

chapters.zh: 不可露你弟兄妻子的下体；这本是你弟兄的下体。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy brother's wife: it thy brother's nakedness.

chapters.zh: 不可露了妇人的下体，又露他女儿的下体，也不可娶他孙女或是外孙女，露他们的下体；他们是骨肉之亲，这本是大恶。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, neither shalt thou take her son's daughter, or her daughter's daughter, to uncover her nakedness; they her near kinswomen: it wickedness.

chapters.zh: 你妻还在的时候，不可另娶他的姊妹作对头，露他的下体。
chapters.en: Neither shalt thou take a wife to her sister, to vex , to uncover her nakedness, beside the other in her life .

chapters.zh: 女人行经不洁净的时候，不可露他的下体，与他亲近。
chapters.en: Also thou shalt not approach unto a woman to uncover her nakedness, as long as she is put apart for her uncleanness.

chapters.zh: 不可与邻舍的妻行淫，玷污自己。
chapters.en: Moreover thou shalt not lie carnally with thy neighbour's wife, to defile thyself with her.

chapters.zh: 不可使你的儿女经火归与摩洛，也不可亵渎你神的名。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And thou shalt not let any of thy seed pass through to Molech, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可与男人苟合，像与女人一样；这本是可憎恶的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not lie with mankind, as with womankind: it abomination.

chapters.zh: 不可与兽淫合，玷污自己。女人也不可站在兽前，与他淫合；这本是逆性的事。
chapters.en: Neither shalt thou lie with any beast to defile thyself therewith: neither shall any woman stand before a beast to lie down thereto: it confusion.

chapters.zh: 在这一切的事上，你们都不可玷污自己；因为我在你们面前所逐出的列邦，在这一切的事上玷污了自己；
chapters.en: Defile not ye yourselves in any of these things: for in all these the nations are defiled which I cast out before you:

chapters.zh: 连地也玷污了，所以我追讨那地的罪孽，那地也吐出他的居民。
chapters.en: And the land is defiled: therefore I do visit the iniquity thereof upon it, and the land itself vomiteth out her inhabitants.

chapters.zh: 故此，你们要守我的律例典章。这一切可憎恶的事，无论是本地人，是寄居在你们中间的外人，都不可行，
chapters.en: Ye shall therefore keep my statutes and my judgments, and shall not commit of these abominations; any of your own nation, nor any stranger that sojourneth among you:

chapters.zh: 在你们以先居住那地的人行了这一切可憎恶的事，地就玷污了，
chapters.en: (For all these abominations have the men of the land done, which before you, and the land is defiled;)

chapters.zh: 免得你们玷污那地的时候，地就把你们吐出，像吐出在你们以先的国民一样。
chapters.en: That the land spue not you out also, when ye defile it, as it spued out the nations that before you.

chapters.zh: 无论甚么人，行了其中可憎的一件事，必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: For whosoever shall commit any of these abominations, even the souls that commit shall be cut off from among their people.

chapters.zh: 所以，你们要守我所吩咐的，免得你们随从那些可憎的恶俗，就是在你们以先的人所常行的，以致玷污了自己。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Therefore shall ye keep mine ordinance, that commit not of these abominable customs, which were committed before you, and that ye defile not yourselves therein: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列全会众说：你们要圣洁，因为我耶和华 ─ 你们的神是圣洁的。
chapters.en: Speak unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, and say unto them, Ye shall be holy: for I the LORD your God holy.

chapters.zh: 你们各人都当孝敬父母，也要守我的安息日。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and keep my sabbaths: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们不可偏向虚无的神，也不可为自己铸造神像。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Turn ye not unto idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们献平安祭给耶和华的时候，要献得可蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD, ye shall offer it at your own will.

chapters.zh: 这祭物要在献的那一天和第二天吃，若有剩到第三天的，就必用火焚烧。
chapters.en: It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the morrow: and if ought remain until the third day, it shall be burnt in the fire.

chapters.zh: 第三天若再吃，这就为可憎恶的，必不蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: And if it be eaten at all on the third day, it abominable; it shall not be accepted.

chapters.zh: 凡吃的人必担当他的罪孽；因为他亵渎了耶和华的圣物，那人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Therefore that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the LORD: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 在你们的地收割庄稼，不可割尽田角，也不可拾取所遗落的。
chapters.en: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly reap the corners of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleanings of thy harvest.

chapters.zh: 不可摘尽葡萄园的果子，也不可拾取葡萄园所掉的果子；要留给穷人和寄居的。我是耶和华你 ─ 们的神。
chapters.en: And thou shalt not glean thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather grape of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and stranger: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们不可偷盗，不可欺骗，也不可彼此说谎。
chapters.en: Ye shall not steal, neither deal falsely, neither lie one to another.

chapters.zh: 不可指著我的名起假誓，亵渎你神的名。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And ye shall not swear by my name falsely, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可欺压你的邻舍，也不可抢夺他的物。雇工人的工价，不可在你那里过夜，留到早晨。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not defraud thy neighbour, neither rob : the wages of him that is hired shall not abide with thee all night until the morning.

chapters.zh: 不可咒骂聋子，也不可将绊脚石放在瞎子面前，只要敬畏你的神。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumblingblock before the blind, but shalt fear thy God: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们施行审判，不可行不义；不可偏护穷人，也不可重看有势力的人，只要按著公义审判你的邻舍。
chapters.en: Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment: thou shalt not respect the person of the poor, nor honour the person of the mighty: in righteousness shalt thou judge thy neighbour.

chapters.zh: 不可在民中往来搬弄是非，也不可与邻舍为敌，置之于死（原文作流他的血）。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not go up and down a talebearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可心里恨你的弟兄；总要指摘你的邻舍，免得因他担罪。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.

chapters.zh: 不可报仇，也不可埋怨你本国的子民，却要爱人如己。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要守我的律例。不可叫你的牲畜与异类配合；不可用两样搀杂的种种你的地，也不可用两样搀杂的料做衣服穿在身上。
chapters.en: Ye shall keep my statutes. Thou shalt not let thy cattle gender with a diverse kind: thou shalt not sow thy field with mingled seed: neither shall a garment mingled of linen and woollen come upon thee.

chapters.zh: 婢女许配了丈夫，还没有被赎、得释放，人若与他行淫，二人要受刑罚，却不把他们治死，因为婢女还没有得自由。
chapters.en: And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, that a bondmaid, betrothed to an husband, and not at all redeemed, nor freedom given her; she shall be scourged; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.

chapters.zh: 那人要把赎愆祭，就是一只公绵羊牵到会幕门口、耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, a ram for a trespass offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司要用赎愆祭的羊在耶和华面前赎他所犯的罪，他的罪就必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering before the LORD for his sin which he hath done: and the sin which he hath done shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 你们到了迦南地，栽种各样结果子的树木，就要以所结的果子如未受割礼的一样。三年之久，你们要以这些果子，如未受割礼的，是不可吃的。
chapters.en: And when ye shall come into the land, and shall have planted all manner of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit thereof as uncircumcised: three years shall it be as uncircumcised unto you: it shall not be eaten of.

chapters.zh: 但第四年所结的果子全要成为圣，用以赞美耶和华。
chapters.en: But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the LORD .

chapters.zh: 第五年，你们要吃那树上的果子，好叫树给你们结果子更多。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们不可吃带血的物；不可用法术，也不可观兆。
chapters.en: Ye shall not eat with the blood: neither shall ye use enchantment, nor observe times.

chapters.zh: 头的周围不可剃，（周围或作：两鬓）胡须的周围也不可损坏。
chapters.en: Ye shall not round the corners of your heads, neither shalt thou mar the corners of thy beard.

chapters.zh: 不可为死人用刀划身，也不可在身上刺花纹。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor print any marks upon you: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可辱没你的女儿，使他为娼妓，恐怕地上的人专向淫乱，地就满了大恶。
chapters.en: Do not prostitute thy daughter, to cause her to be a whore; lest the land fall to whoredom, and the land become full of wickedness.

chapters.zh: 你们要守我的安息日，敬我的圣所。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可偏向那些交鬼的和行巫术的；不可求问他们，以致被他们玷污了。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Regard not them that have familiar spirits, neither seek after wizards, to be defiled by them: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 在白发的人面前，你要站起来；也要尊敬老人，又要敬畏你的神。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Thou shalt rise up before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and fear thy God: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 若有外人在你们国中和你同居，就不可欺负他。
chapters.en: And if a stranger sojourn with thee in your land, ye shall not vex him.

chapters.zh: 和你们同居的外人，你们要看他如本地人一样，并要爱他如己，因为你们在埃及地也作过寄居的。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: the stranger that dwelleth with you shall be unto you as one born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们施行审判，不可行不义；在尺、秤、升、斗上也是如此。
chapters.en: Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.

chapters.zh: 要用公道天平、公道法码、公道升斗、公道秤。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神，曾把你们从埃及地领出来的。
chapters.en: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 你们要谨守遵行我一切的律例典章。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你还要晓谕以色列人说：凡以色列人，或是在以色列中寄居的外人，把自己的儿女献给摩洛的，总要治死他；本地人要用石头把他打死。
chapters.en: Again, thou shalt say to the children of Israel, Whosoever of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn in Israel, that giveth of his seed unto Molech; he shall surely be put to death: the people of the land shall stone him with stones.

chapters.zh: 我也要向那人变脸，把他从民中剪除；因为他把儿女献给摩洛，玷污我的圣所，亵渎我的圣名。
chapters.en: And I will set my face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people; because he hath given of his seed unto Molech, to defile my sanctuary, and to profane my holy name.

chapters.zh: 那人把儿女献给摩洛，本地人若佯为不见，不把他治死，
chapters.en: And if the people of the land do any ways hide their eyes from the man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech, and kill him not:

chapters.zh: 我就要向这人和他的家变脸，把他和一切随他与摩洛行邪淫的人都从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Then I will set my face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that go a whoring after him, to commit whoredom with Molech, from among their people.

chapters.zh: 人偏向交鬼的和行巫术的，随他们行邪淫，我要向那人变脸，把他从民中剪除。
chapters.en: And the soul that turneth after such as have familiar spirits, and after wizards, to go a whoring after them, I will even set my face against that soul, and will cut him off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 所以你们要自洁成圣，因为我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Sanctify yourselves therefore, and be ye holy: for I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们要谨守遵行我的律例；我是叫你们成圣的耶和华。
chapters.en: And ye shall keep my statutes, and do them: I the LORD which sanctify you.

chapters.zh: 凡咒骂父母的，总要治死他；他咒骂了父母，他的罪要归到他身上。（罪原文作血；本章同）
chapters.en: For every one that curseth his father or his mother shall be surely put to death: he hath cursed his father or his mother; his blood upon him.

chapters.zh: 与邻舍之妻行淫的，奸夫淫妇都必治死。
chapters.en: And the man that committeth adultery with man's wife, that committeth adultery with his neighbour's wife, the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 与继母行淫的，就是羞辱了他父亲，总要把他们二人治死，罪要归到他们身上。
chapters.en: And the man that lieth with his father's wife hath uncovered his father's nakedness: both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood upon them.

chapters.zh: 与儿妇同房的，总要把他们二人治死；他们行了逆伦的事，罪要归到他们身上。
chapters.en: And if a man lie with his daughter in law, both of them shall surely be put to death: they have wrought confusion; their blood upon them.

chapters.zh: 人若与男人苟合，像与女人一样，他们二人行了可憎的事，总要把他们治死，罪要归到他们身上。
chapters.en: If a man also lie with mankind, as he lieth with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination: they shall surely be put to death; their blood upon them.

chapters.zh: 人若娶妻，并娶其母，便是大恶，要把这三人用火焚烧，使你们中间免去大恶。
chapters.en: And if a man take a wife and her mother, it wickedness: they shall be burnt with fire, both he and they; that there be no wickedness among you.

chapters.zh: 人若与兽淫合，总要治死他，也要杀那兽。
chapters.en: And if a man lie with a beast, he shall surely be put to death: and ye shall slay the beast.

chapters.zh: 女人若与兽亲近，与他淫合，你要杀那女人和那兽，总要把他们治死，罪要归到他们身上。
chapters.en: And if a woman approach unto any beast, and lie down thereto, thou shalt kill the woman, and the beast: they shall surely be put to death; their blood upon them.

chapters.zh: 人若娶他的姊妹，无论是异母同父的，是异父同母的，彼此见了下体，这是可耻的事；他们必在本民的眼前被剪除。他露了姊妹的下体，必担当自己的罪孽。
chapters.en: And if a man shall take his sister, his father's daughter, or his mother's daughter, and see her nakedness, and she see his nakedness; it a wicked thing; and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people: he hath uncovered his sister's nakedness; he shall bear his iniquity.

chapters.zh: 妇人有月经，若与他同房，露了他的下体，就是露了妇人的血源，妇人也露了自己的血源，二人必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: And if a man shall lie with a woman having her sickness, and shall uncover her nakedness; he hath discovered her fountain, and she hath uncovered the fountain of her blood: and both of them shall be cut off from among their people.

chapters.zh: 不可露姨母或是姑母的下体，这是露了骨肉之亲的下体；二人必担当自己的罪孽。
chapters.en: And thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother's sister, nor of thy father's sister: for he uncovereth his near kin: they shall bear their iniquity.

chapters.zh: 人若与伯叔之妻同房，就羞辱了他的伯叔；二人要担当自己的罪，必无子女而死。
chapters.en: And if a man shall lie with his uncle's wife, he hath uncovered his uncle's nakedness: they shall bear their sin; they shall die childless.

chapters.zh: 人若娶弟兄之妻，这本是污秽的事，羞辱了他的弟兄；二人必无子女。
chapters.en: And if a man shall take his brother's wife, it an unclean thing: he hath uncovered his brother's nakedness; they shall be childless.

chapters.zh: 所以，你们要谨守遵行我一切的律例典章，免得我领你们去住的那地把你们吐出。
chapters.en: Ye shall therefore keep all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, spue you not out.

chapters.zh: 我在你们面前所逐出的国民，你们不可随从他们的风俗；因为他们行了这一切的事，所以我厌恶他们。
chapters.en: And ye shall not walk in the manners of the nation, which I cast out before you: for they committed all these things, and therefore I abhorred them.

chapters.zh: 但我对你们说过，你们要承受他们的地，就是我要赐给你们为业、流奶与蜜之地。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神，使你们与万民有分别的。
chapters.en: But I have said unto you, Ye shall inherit their land, and I will give it unto you to possess it, a land that floweth with milk and honey: I the LORD your God, which have separated you from people.

chapters.zh: 所以，你们要把洁净和不洁净的禽兽分别出来；不可因我给你们分为不洁净的禽兽，或是滋生在地上的活物，使自己成为可憎恶的。
chapters.en: Ye shall therefore put difference between clean beasts and unclean, and between unclean fowls and clean: and ye shall not make your souls abominable by beast, or by fowl, or by any manner of living thing that creepeth on the ground, which I have separated from you as unclean.

chapters.zh: 你们要归我为圣，因为我 ─ 耶和华是圣的，并叫你们与万民有分别，使你们作我的民。
chapters.en: And ye shall be holy unto me: for I the LORD holy, and have severed you from people, that ye should be mine.

chapters.zh: 无论男女，是交鬼的或行巫术的，总要治死他们。人必用石头把他们打死，罪要归到他们身上。
chapters.en: A man also or woman that hath a familiar spirit, or that is a wizard, shall surely be put to death: they shall stone them with stones: their blood upon them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你告诉亚伦子孙作祭司的说：祭司不可为民中的死人沾染自己，
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Speak unto the priests the sons of Aaron, and say unto them, There shall none be defiled for the dead among his people:

chapters.zh: 除非为他骨肉之亲的父母、儿女、弟兄，
chapters.en: But for his kin, that is near unto him, , for his mother, and for his father, and for his son, and for his daughter, and for his brother,

chapters.zh: 和未曾出嫁、作处女的姊妹，才可以沾染自己。
chapters.en: And for his sister a virgin, that is nigh unto him, which hath had no husband; for her may he be defiled.

chapters.zh: 祭司既在民中为首，就不可从俗沾染自己。
chapters.en: he shall not defile himself, a chief man among his people, to profane himself.

chapters.zh: 不可使头光秃；不可剃除胡须的周围，也不可用刀划身。
chapters.en: They shall not make baldness upon their head, neither shall they shave off the corner of their beard, nor make any cuttings in their flesh.

chapters.zh: 要归神为圣，不可亵渎神的名；因为耶和华的火祭，就是神的食物，是他们献的，所以他们要成为圣。
chapters.en: They shall be holy unto their God, and not profane the name of their God: for the offerings of the LORD made by fire, the bread of their God, they do offer: therefore they shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 不可娶妓女或被污的女人为妻，也不可娶被休的妇人为妻，因为祭司是归神为圣。
chapters.en: They shall not take a wife a whore, or profane; neither shall they take a woman put away from her husband: for he holy unto his God.

chapters.zh: 所以你要使他成圣，因为他奉献你神的食物；你要以他为圣，因为我 ─ 使你们成圣的耶和华 ─ 是圣的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt sanctify him therefore; for he offereth the bread of thy God: he shall be holy unto thee: for I the LORD, which sanctify you, holy.

chapters.zh: 祭司的女儿若行淫辱没自己，就辱没了父亲，必用火将他焚烧。
chapters.en: And the daughter of any priest, if she profane herself by playing the whore, she profaneth her father: she shall be burnt with fire.

chapters.zh: 在弟兄中作大祭司、头上倒了膏油、又承接圣职，穿了圣衣的，不可蓬头散发，也不可撕裂衣服。
chapters.en: And the high priest among his brethren, upon whose head the anointing oil was poured, and that is consecrated to put on the garments, shall not uncover his head, nor rend his clothes;

chapters.zh: 不可挨近死尸，也不可为父母沾染自己。
chapters.en: Neither shall he go in to any dead body, nor defile himself for his father, or for his mother;

chapters.zh: 不可出圣所，也不可亵渎神的圣所，因为神膏油的冠冕在他头上。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the crown of the anointing oil of his God upon him: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他要娶处女为妻。
chapters.en: And he shall take a wife in her virginity.

chapters.zh: 寡妇或是被休的妇人，或是被污为妓的女人，都不可娶；只可娶本民中的处女为妻。
chapters.en: A widow, or a divorced woman, or profane, an harlot, these shall he not take: but he shall take a virgin of his own people to wife.

chapters.zh: 不可在民中辱没他的儿女，因为我是叫他成圣的耶和华。
chapters.en: Neither shall he profane his seed among his people: for I the LORD do sanctify him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你告诉亚伦说：你世世代代的后裔，凡有残疾的，都不可近前来献他神的食物。
chapters.en: Speak unto Aaron, saying, Whosoever of thy seed in their generations that hath blemish, let him not approach to offer the bread of his God.

chapters.zh: 因为凡有残疾的，无论是瞎眼的、瘸腿的、塌鼻子的、肢体有余的、
chapters.en: For whatsoever man that hath a blemish, he shall not approach: a blind man, or a lame, or he that hath a flat nose, or any thing superfluous,

chapters.zh: 折脚折手的、
chapters.en: Or a man that is brokenfooted, or brokenhanded,

chapters.zh: 驼背的、矮矬的、眼睛有毛病的、长癣的、长疥的，或是损坏肾子的，都不可近前来。
chapters.en: Or crookbackt, or a dwarf, or that hath a blemish in his eye, or be scurvy, or scabbed, or hath his stones broken;

chapters.zh: 祭司亚伦的后裔，凡有残疾的，都不可近前来，将火祭献给耶和华。他有残疾，不可近前来献神的食物。
chapters.en: No man that hath a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest shall come nigh to offer the offerings of the LORD made by fire: he hath a blemish; he shall not come nigh to offer the bread of his God.

chapters.zh: 神的食物，无论是圣的，至圣的，他都可以吃。
chapters.en: He shall eat the bread of his God, of the most holy, and of the holy.

chapters.zh: 但不可进到幔子前，也不可就近坛前；因为他有残疾，免得亵渎我的圣所。我是叫他成圣的耶和华。
chapters.en: Only he shall not go in unto the vail, nor come nigh unto the altar, because he hath a blemish; that he profane not my sanctuaries: for I the LORD do sanctify them.

chapters.zh: 于是，摩西晓谕亚伦和亚伦的子孙，并以色列众人。
chapters.en: And Moses told unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你吩咐亚伦和他子孙说：要远离以色列人所分别为圣、归给我的圣物，免得亵渎我的圣名。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name which they hallow unto me: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你要对他们说：你们世世代代的后裔，凡身上有污秽、亲近以色列人所分别为圣、归耶和华圣物的，那人必在我面前剪除。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Say unto them, Whosoever of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from my presence: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的后裔，凡长大痲疯的，或是有漏症的，不可吃圣物，直等他洁净了。无论谁摸那因死尸不洁净的物（物或作：人），或是遗精的人，
chapters.en: What man soever of the seed of Aaron a leper, or hath a running issue; he shall not eat of the holy things, until he be clean. And whoso toucheth any thing unclean the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;

chapters.zh: 或是摸甚么使他不洁净的爬物，或是摸那使他不洁净的人（不拘那人有甚么不洁净），
chapters.en: Or whosoever toucheth any creeping thing, whereby he may be made unclean, or a man of whom he may take uncleanness, whatsoever uncleanness he hath;

chapters.zh: 摸了这些人、物的，必不洁净到晚上；若不用水洗身，就不可吃圣物。
chapters.en: The soul which hath touched any such shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he wash his flesh with water.

chapters.zh: 日落的时候，他就洁净了，然后可以吃圣物，因为这是他的食物。
chapters.en: And when the sun is down, he shall be clean, and shall afterward eat of the holy things; because it his food.

chapters.zh: 自死的或是被野兽撕裂的，他不可吃，因此污秽自己。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: That which dieth of itself, or is torn , he shall not eat to defile himself therewith: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 所以他们要守我所吩咐的，免得轻忽了，因此担罪而死。我是叫他们成圣的耶和华。
chapters.en: They shall therefore keep mine ordinance, lest they bear sin for it, and die therefore, if they profane it: I the LORD do sanctify them.

chapters.zh: 凡外人不可吃圣物；寄居在祭司家的，或是雇工人，都不可吃圣物；
chapters.en: There shall no stranger eat the holy thing: a sojourner of the priest, or an hired servant, shall not eat the holy thing.

chapters.zh: 倘若祭司买人，是他的钱买的，那人就可以吃圣物；生在他家的人也可以吃。
chapters.en: But if the priest buy soul with his money, he shall eat of it, and he that is born in his house: they shall eat of his meat.

chapters.zh: 祭司的女儿若嫁外人，就不可吃举祭的圣物。
chapters.en: If the priest's daughter also be unto a stranger, she may not eat of an offering of the holy things.

chapters.zh: 但祭司的女儿若是寡妇，或是被休的，没有孩子，又归回父家，与他青年一样，就可以吃他父亲的食物；只是外人不可吃。
chapters.en: But if the priest's daughter be a widow, or divorced, and have no child, and is returned unto her father's house, as in her youth, she shall eat of her father's meat: but there shall no stranger eat thereof.

chapters.zh: 若有人误吃了圣物，要照圣物的原数加上五分之一交给祭司。
chapters.en: And if a man eat the holy thing unwittingly, then he shall put the fifth thereof unto it, and shall give unto the priest with the holy thing.

chapters.zh: 祭司不可亵渎以色列人所献给耶和华的圣物，
chapters.en: And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer unto the LORD;

chapters.zh: 免得他们在吃圣物上自取罪孽，因为我是叫他们成圣的耶和华。
chapters.en: Or suffer them to bear the iniquity of trespass, when they eat their holy things: for I the LORD do sanctify them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕亚伦和他子孙，并以色列众人说：以色列家中的人，或在以色列中寄居的，凡献供物，无论是所许的愿，是甘心献的，就是献给耶和华作燔祭的，
chapters.en: Speak unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them, Whatsoever of the house of Israel, or of the strangers in Israel, that will offer his oblation for all his vows, and for all his freewill offerings, which they will offer unto the LORD for a burnt offering;

chapters.zh: 要将没有残疾的公牛，或是绵羊，或是山羊献上，如此方蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: at your own will a male without blemish, of the beeves, of the sheep, or of the goats.

chapters.zh: 凡有残疾的，你们不可献上，因为这不蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: whatsoever hath a blemish, shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.

chapters.zh: 凡从牛群或是羊群中，将平安祭献给耶和华，为要还特许的愿，或是作甘心献的，所献的必纯全无残疾的才蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: And whosoever offereth a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD to accomplish vow, or a freewill offering in beeves or sheep, it shall be perfect to be accepted; there shall be no blemish therein.

chapters.zh: 瞎眼的、折伤的、残废的、有瘤子的、长癣的、长疥的都不可献给耶和华，也不可在坛上作为火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto the LORD, nor make an offering by fire of them upon the altar unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 无论是公牛是绵羊羔，若肢体有余的，或是缺少的，只可作甘心祭献上；用以还愿，却不蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: Either a bullock or a lamb that hath any thing superfluous or lacking in his parts, that mayest thou offer a freewill offering; but for a vow it shall not be accepted.

chapters.zh: 肾子损伤的，或是压碎的，或是破裂的，或是骟了的，不可献给耶和华，在你们的地上也不可这样行。
chapters.en: Ye shall not offer unto the LORD that which is bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut; neither shall ye make in your land.

chapters.zh: 这类的物，你们从外人的手，一样也不可接受作你们神的食物献上；因为这些都有损坏，有残疾，不蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: Neither from a stranger's hand shall ye offer the bread of your God of any of these; because their corruption in them, blemishes in them: they shall not be accepted for you.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 才生的公牛，或是绵羊或是山羊，七天当跟著母；从第八天以后，可以当供物蒙悦纳，作为耶和华的火祭。
chapters.en: When a bullock, or a sheep, or a goat, is brought forth, then it shall be seven days under the dam; and from the eighth day and thenceforth it shall be accepted for an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 无论是母牛是母羊，不可同日宰母和子。
chapters.en: And cow or ewe, ye shall not kill it and her young both in one day.

chapters.zh: 你们献感谢祭给耶和华，要献得可蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: And when ye will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto the LORD, offer at your own will.

chapters.zh: 要当天吃，一点不可留到早晨。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: On the same day it shall be eaten up; ye shall leave none of it until the morrow: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要谨守遵行我的诫命。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们不可亵渎我的圣名；我在以色列人中，却要被尊为圣。我是叫你们成圣的耶和华，
chapters.en: Neither shall ye profane my holy name; but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I the LORD which hallow you,

chapters.zh: 把你们从埃及地领出来，作你们的神。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: That brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：耶和华的节期，你们要宣告为圣会的节期。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim holy convocations, these my feasts.

chapters.zh: 六日要做工，第七日是圣安息日，当有圣会；你们甚么工都不可做。这是在你们一切的住处向耶和华守的安息日。
chapters.en: Six days shall work be done: but the seventh day the sabbath of rest, an holy convocation; ye shall do no work : it the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的节期，就是你们到了日期要宣告为圣会的，乃是这些。
chapters.en: These the feasts of the LORD, holy convocations, which ye shall proclaim in their seasons.

chapters.zh: 正月十四日，黄昏的时候，是耶和华的逾越节。
chapters.en: In the fourteenth of the first month at even the LORD'S passover.

chapters.zh: 这月十五日是向耶和华守的无酵节；你们要吃无酵饼七日。
chapters.en: And on the fifteenth day of the same month the feast of unleavened bread unto the LORD: seven days ye must eat unleavened bread.

chapters.zh: 第一日当有圣会，甚么劳碌的工都不可做；
chapters.en: In the first day ye shall have an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

chapters.zh: 要将火祭献给耶和华七日。第七日是圣会，甚么劳碌的工都不可做。
chapters.en: But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD seven days: in the seventh day an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work .

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：你们到了我赐给你们的地，收割庄稼的时候，要将初熟的庄稼一捆带给祭司。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land which I give unto you, and shall reap the harvest thereof, then ye shall bring a sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest unto the priest:

chapters.zh: 他要把这一捆在耶和华面前摇一摇，使你们得蒙悦纳。祭司要在安息日的次日把这捆摇一摇。
chapters.en: And he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD, to be accepted for you: on the morrow after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.

chapters.zh: 摇这捆的日子，你们要把一岁、没有残疾的公绵羊羔献给耶和华为燔祭。
chapters.en: And ye shall offer that day when ye wave the sheaf an he lamb without blemish of the first year for a burnt offering unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 同献的素祭，就是调油的细面伊法十分之二，作为馨香的火祭，献给耶和华。同献的奠祭，要酒一欣四分之一。
chapters.en: And the meat offering thereof two tenth deals of fine flour mingled with oil, an offering made by fire unto the LORD a sweet savour: and the drink offering thereof of wine, the fourth of an hin.

chapters.zh: 无论是饼，是烘的子粒，是新穗子，你们都不可吃，直等到把你们献给神的供物带来的那一天才可以吃。这在你们一切的住处作为世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: And ye shall eat neither bread, nor parched corn, nor green ears, until the selfsame day that ye have brought an offering unto your God: a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.

chapters.zh: 你们要从安息日的次日，献禾捆为摇祭的那日算起，要满了七个安息日。
chapters.en: And ye shall count unto you from the morrow after the sabbath, from the day that ye brought the sheaf of the wave offering; seven sabbaths shall be complete:

chapters.zh: 到第七个安息日的次日，共计五十天，又要将新素祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Even unto the morrow after the seventh sabbath shall ye number fifty days; and ye shall offer a new meat offering unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 要从你们的住处取出细面伊法十分之二，加酵，烤成两个摇祭的饼，当作初熟之物献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Ye shall bring out of your habitations two wave loaves of two tenth deals: they shall be of fine flour; they shall be baken with leaven; the firstfruits unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 又要将一岁、没有残疾的羊羔七只、公牛犊一只、公绵羊两只，和饼一同奉上。这些与同献的素祭和奠祭要作为燔祭献给耶和华，就是作馨香的火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: And ye shall offer with the bread seven lambs without blemish of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams: they shall be a burnt offering unto the LORD, with their meat offering, and their drink offerings, an offering made by fire, of sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要献一只公山羊为赎罪祭，两只一岁的公绵羊羔为平安祭。
chapters.en: Then ye shall sacrifice one kid of the goats for a sin offering, and two lambs of the first year for a sacrifice of peace offerings.

chapters.zh: 祭司要把这些和初熟麦子做的饼一同作摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇；这是献与耶和华为圣物归给祭司的。
chapters.en: And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits a wave offering before the LORD, with the two lambs: they shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.

chapters.zh: 当这日，你们要宣告圣会；甚么劳碌的工都不可做。这在你们一切的住处作为世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: And ye shall proclaim on the selfsame day, it may be an holy convocation unto you: ye shall do no servile work a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.

chapters.zh: 在你们的地收割庄稼，不可割尽田角，也不可拾取所遗落的；要留给穷人和寄居的。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not make clean riddance of the corners of thy field when thou reapest, neither shalt thou gather any gleaning of thy harvest: thou shalt leave them unto the poor, and to the stranger: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：七月初一，你们要守为圣安息日，要吹角作纪念，当有圣会。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, In the seventh month, in the first of the month, shall ye have a sabbath, a memorial of blowing of trumpets, an holy convocation.

chapters.zh: 甚么劳碌的工都不可做；要将火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Ye shall do no servile work : but ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 七月初十是赎罪日；你们要守为圣会，并要刻苦己心，也要将火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Also on the tenth of this seventh month a day of atonement: it shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 当这日，甚么工都不可做；因为是赎罪日，要在耶和华 ─ 你们的神面前赎罪。
chapters.en: And ye shall do no work in that same day: for it a day of atonement, to make an atonement for you before the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 当这日，凡不刻苦己心的，必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: For whatsoever soul that shall not be afflicted in that same day, he shall be cut off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 凡这日做甚么工的，我必将他从民中除灭。
chapters.en: And whatsoever soul that doeth any work in that same day, the same soul will I destroy from among his people.

chapters.zh: 你们甚么工都不可做。这在你们一切的住处作为世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: Ye shall do no manner of work: a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.

chapters.zh: 你们要守这日为圣安息日，并要刻苦己心。从这月初九日晚上到次日晚上，要守为安息日。
chapters.en: It unto you a sabbath of rest, and ye shall afflict your souls: in the ninth of the month at even, from even unto even, shall ye celebrate your sabbath.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：这七月十五日是住棚节，要在耶和华面前守这节七日。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, The fifteenth day of this seventh month the feast of tabernacles seven days unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 第一日当有圣会，甚么劳碌的工都不可做。
chapters.en: On the first day an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work .

chapters.zh: 七日内要将火祭献给耶和华。第八日当守圣会，要将火祭献给耶和华。这是严肃会，甚么劳碌的工都不可做。
chapters.en: Seven days ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: on the eighth day shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it a solemn assembly; ye shall do no servile work .

chapters.zh: 这是耶和华的节期，就是你们要宣告为圣会的节期；要将火祭、燔祭、素祭、祭物，并奠祭，各归各日，献给耶和华。
chapters.en: These the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim holy convocations, to offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD, a burnt offering, and a meat offering, a sacrifice, and drink offerings, every thing upon his day:

chapters.zh: 这是在耶和华的安息日以外，又在你们的供物和所许的愿，并甘心献给耶和华的以外。
chapters.en: Beside the sabbaths of the LORD, and beside your gifts, and beside all your vows, and beside all your freewill offerings, which ye give unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们收藏了地的出产，就从七月十五日起，要守耶和华的节七日。第一日为圣安息；第八日也为圣安息。
chapters.en: Also in the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the fruit of the land, ye shall keep a feast unto the LORD seven days: on the first day a sabbath, and on the eighth day a sabbath.

chapters.zh: 第一日要拿美好树上的果子和棕树上的枝子，与茂密树的枝条并河旁的柳枝，在耶和华 ─ 你们的神面前欢乐七日。
chapters.en: And ye shall take you on the first day the boughs of goodly trees, branches of palm trees, and the boughs of thick trees, and willows of the brook; and ye shall rejoice before the LORD your God seven days.

chapters.zh: 每年七月间，要向耶和华守这节七日。这为你们世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: And ye shall keep it a feast unto the LORD seven days in the year. a statute for ever in your generations: ye shall celebrate it in the seventh month.

chapters.zh: 你们要住在棚里七日；凡以色列家的人都要住在棚里，
chapters.en: Ye shall dwell in booths seven days; all that are Israelites born shall dwell in booths:

chapters.zh: 好叫你们世世代代知道，我领以色列人出埃及地的时候曾使他们住在棚里。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: That your generations may know that I made the children of Israel to dwell in booths, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 于是，摩西将耶和华的节期传给以色列人。
chapters.en: And Moses declared unto the children of Israel the feasts of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 要吩咐以色列人，把那为点灯捣成的清橄榄油拿来给你，使灯常常点著。
chapters.en: Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamps to burn continually.

chapters.zh: 在会幕中法柜的幔子外，亚伦从晚上到早晨必在耶和华面前经理这灯。这要作你们世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: Without the vail of the testimony, in the tabernacle of the congregation, shall Aaron order it from the evening unto the morning before the LORD continually: a statute for ever in your generations.

chapters.zh: 他要在耶和华面前常收拾精金灯台上的灯。
chapters.en: He shall order the lamps upon the pure candlestick before the LORD continually.

chapters.zh: 你要取细面，烤成十二个饼，每饼用面伊法十分之二。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take fine flour, and bake twelve cakes thereof: two tenth deals shall be in one cake.

chapters.zh: 要把饼摆列两行（或作：摞；下同），每行六个，在耶和华面前精金的桌子上；
chapters.en: And thou shalt set them in two rows, six on a row, upon the pure table before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 又要把净乳香放在每行饼上，作为纪念，就是作为火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put pure frankincense upon row, that it may be on the bread for a memorial, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 每安息日要常摆在耶和华面前；这为以色列人作永远的约。
chapters.en: Every sabbath he shall set it in order before the LORD continually, from the children of Israel by an everlasting covenant.

chapters.zh: 这饼是要给亚伦和他子孙的；他们要在圣处吃，为永远的定例，因为在献给耶和华的火祭中是至圣的。
chapters.en: And it shall be Aaron's and his sons'; and they shall eat it in the holy place: for it most holy unto him of the offerings of the LORD made by fire by a perpetual statute.

chapters.zh: 有一个以色列妇人的儿子，他父亲是埃及人，一日闲游在以色列人中。这以色列妇人的儿子和一个以色列人在营里争斗。
chapters.en: And the son of an Israelitish woman, whose father an Egyptian, went out among the children of Israel: and this son of the Israelitish and a man of Israel strove together in the camp;

chapters.zh: 这以色列妇人的儿子亵渎了圣名，并且咒诅，就有人把他送到摩西那里。（他母亲名叫示罗密，是但支派底伯利的女儿。）
chapters.en: And the Israelitish woman's son blasphemed the name , and cursed. And they brought him unto Moses: (and his mother's name Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan:)

chapters.zh: 他们把那人收在监里，要得耶和华所指示的话。
chapters.en: And they put him in ward, that the mind of the LORD might be shewed them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 把那咒诅圣名的人带到营外。叫听见的人都放手在他头上；全会众就要用石头打死他。
chapters.en: Bring forth him that hath cursed without the camp; and let all that heard lay their hands upon his head, and let all the congregation stone him.

chapters.zh: 你要晓谕以色列人说：凡咒诅神的，必担当他的罪。
chapters.en: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Whosoever curseth his God shall bear his sin.

chapters.zh: 那亵渎耶和华名的，必被治死；全会众总要用石头打死他。不管是寄居的是本地人，他亵渎耶和华名的时候，必被治死。
chapters.en: And he that blasphemeth the name of the LORD, he shall surely be put to death, all the congregation shall certainly stone him: as well the stranger, as he that is born in the land, when he blasphemeth the name , shall be put to death.

chapters.zh: 打死人的，必被治死；
chapters.en: And he that killeth any man shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 打死牲畜的，必赔上牲畜，以命偿命。
chapters.en: And he that killeth a beast shall make it good; beast for beast.

chapters.zh: 人若使他邻舍的身体有残疾，他怎样行，也要照样向他行：
chapters.en: And if a man cause a blemish in his neighbour; as he hath done, so shall it be done to him;

chapters.zh: 以伤还伤，以眼还眼，以牙还牙。他怎样叫人的身体有残疾，也要照样向他行。
chapters.en: Breach for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth: as he hath caused a blemish in a man, so shall it be done to him .

chapters.zh: 打死牲畜的，必赔上牲畜；打死人的，必被治死。
chapters.en: And he that killeth a beast, he shall restore it: and he that killeth a man, he shall be put to death.

chapters.zh: 不管是寄居的是本地人，同归一例。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Ye shall have one manner of law, as well for the stranger, as for one of your own country: for I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 于是，摩西晓谕以色列人，他们就把那咒诅圣名的人带到营外，用石头打死。以色列人就照耶和华所吩咐摩西的行了。
chapters.en: And Moses spake to the children of Israel, that they should bring forth him that had cursed out of the camp, and stone him with stones. And the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在西乃山对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses in mount Sinai, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：你们到了我所赐你们那地的时候，地就要向耶和华守安息。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land which I give you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 六年要耕种田地，也要修理葡萄园，收藏地的出产。
chapters.en: Six years thou shalt sow thy field, and six years thou shalt prune thy vineyard, and gather in the fruit thereof;

chapters.zh: 第七年，地要守圣安息，就是向耶和华守的安息，不可耕种田地，也不可修理葡萄园。
chapters.en: But in the seventh year shall be a sabbath of rest unto the land, a sabbath for the LORD: thou shalt neither sow thy field, nor prune thy vineyard.

chapters.zh: 遗落自长的庄稼不可收割；没有修理的葡萄树也不可摘取葡萄。这年，地要守圣安息。
chapters.en: That which groweth of its own accord of thy harvest thou shalt not reap, neither gather the grapes of thy vine undressed: it is a year of rest unto the land.

chapters.zh: 地在安息年所出的，要给你和你的仆人、婢女、雇工人，并寄居的外人当食物。
chapters.en: And the sabbath of the land shall be meat for you; for thee, and for thy servant, and for thy maid, and for thy hired servant, and for thy stranger that sojourneth with thee,

chapters.zh: 这年的土产也要给你的牲畜和你地上的走兽当食物。
chapters.en: And for thy cattle, and for the beast that in thy land, shall all the increase thereof be meat.

chapters.zh: 你要计算七个安息年，就是七七年。这便为你成了七个安息年，共是四十九年。
chapters.en: And thou shalt number seven sabbaths of years unto thee, seven times seven years; and the space of the seven sabbaths of years shall be unto thee forty and nine years.

chapters.zh: 当年七月初十日，你要大发角声；这日就是赎罪日，要在遍地发出角声。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou cause the trumpet of the jubile to sound on the tenth of the seventh month, in the day of atonement shall ye make the trumpet sound throughout all your land.

chapters.zh: 第五十年，你们要当作圣年，在遍地给一切的居民宣告自由。这年必为你们的禧年，各人要归自己的产业，各归本家。
chapters.en: And ye shall hallow the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout the land unto all the inhabitants thereof: it shall be a jubile unto you; and ye shall return every man unto his possession, and ye shall return every man unto his family.

chapters.zh: 第五十年要作为你们的禧年。这年不可耕种；地中自长的，不可收割；没有修理的葡萄树也不可摘取葡萄。
chapters.en: A jubile shall that fiftieth year be unto you: ye shall not sow, neither reap that which groweth of itself in it, nor gather in it of thy vine undressed.

chapters.zh: 因为这是禧年，你们要当作圣年，吃地中自出的土产。
chapters.en: For it the jubile; it shall be holy unto you: ye shall eat the increase thereof out of the field.

chapters.zh: 这禧年，你们各人要归自己的地业。
chapters.en: In the year of this jubile ye shall return every man unto his possession.

chapters.zh: 你若卖甚么给邻舍，或是从邻舍的手中买甚么，彼此不可亏负。
chapters.en: And if thou sell ought unto thy neighbour, or buyest of thy neighbour's hand, ye shall not oppress one another:

chapters.zh: 你要按禧年以后的年数向邻舍买；他也要按年数的收成卖给你。
chapters.en: According to the number of years after the jubile thou shalt buy of thy neighbour, according unto the number of years of the fruits he shall sell unto thee:

chapters.zh: 年岁若多，要照数加添价值；年岁若少，要照数减去价值，因为他照收成的数目卖给你。
chapters.en: According to the multitude of years thou shalt increase the price thereof, and according to the fewness of years thou shalt diminish the price of it: for to the number of the fruits doth he sell unto thee.

chapters.zh: 你们彼此不可亏负，只要敬畏你们的神，因为我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Ye shall not therefore oppress one another; but thou shalt fear thy God: for I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 我的律例，你们要遵行，我的典章，你们要谨守，就可以在那地上安然居住。
chapters.en: Wherefore ye shall do my statutes, and keep my judgments, and do them; and ye shall dwell in the land in safety.

chapters.zh: 地必出土产，你们就要吃饱，在那地上安然居住。
chapters.en: And the land shall yield her fruit, and ye shall eat your fill, and dwell therein in safety.

chapters.zh: 你们若说：这第七年我们不耕种，也不收藏土产，吃甚么呢？
chapters.en: And if ye shall say, What shall we eat the seventh year? behold, we shall not sow, nor gather in our increase:

chapters.zh: 我必在第六年将我所命的福赐给你们，地便生三年的土产。
chapters.en: Then I will command my blessing upon you in the sixth year, and it shall bring forth fruit for three years.

chapters.zh: 第八年，你们要耕种，也要吃陈粮，等到第九年出产收来的时候，你们还吃陈粮。
chapters.en: And ye shall sow the eighth year, and eat of old fruit until the ninth year; until her fruits come in ye shall eat the old .

chapters.zh: 地不可永卖，因为地是我的；你们在我面前是客旅，是寄居的。
chapters.en: The land shall not be sold for ever: for the land mine; for ye strangers and sojourners with me.

chapters.zh: 在你们所得为业的全地，也要准人将地赎回。
chapters.en: And in all the land of your possession ye shall grant a redemption for the land.

chapters.zh: 你的弟兄（弟兄是指本国人说；下同）若渐渐穷乏，卖了几分地业，他至近的亲属就要来把弟兄所卖的赎回。
chapters.en: If thy brother be waxen poor, and hath sold away of his possession, and if any of his kin come to redeem it, then shall he redeem that which his brother sold.

chapters.zh: 若没有能给他赎回的，他自己渐渐富足，能够赎回，
chapters.en: And if the man have none to redeem it, and himself be able to redeem it;

chapters.zh: 就要算出卖地的年数，把余剩年数的价值还那买主，自己便归回自己的地业。
chapters.en: Then let him count the years of the sale thereof, and restore the overplus unto the man to whom he sold it; that he may return unto his possession.

chapters.zh: 倘若不能为自己得回所卖的，仍要存在买主的手里直到禧年；到了禧年，地业要出买主的手，自己便归回自己的地业。
chapters.en: But if he be not able to restore to him, then that which is sold shall remain in the hand of him that hath bought it until the year of jubile: and in the jubile it shall go out, and he shall return unto his possession.

chapters.zh: 人若卖城内的住宅，卖了以后，一年之内可以赎回；在一整年，必有赎回的权柄。
chapters.en: And if a man sell a dwelling house in a walled city, then he may redeem it within a whole year after it is sold; a full year may he redeem it.

chapters.zh: 若在一整年之内不赎回，这城内的房屋就定准永归买主，世世代代为业；在禧年也不得出买主的手。
chapters.en: And if it be not redeemed within the space of a full year, then the house that in the walled city shall be established for ever to him that bought it throughout his generations: it shall not go out in the jubile.

chapters.zh: 但房屋在无城墙的村庄里，要看如乡下的田地一样，可以赎回；到了禧年，都要出买主的手。
chapters.en: But the houses of the villages which have no wall round about them shall be counted as the fields of the country: they may be redeemed, and they shall go out in the jubile.

chapters.zh: 然而利未人所得为业的城邑，其中的房屋，利未人可以随时赎回。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding the cities of the Levites, the houses of the cities of their possession, may the Levites redeem at any time.

chapters.zh: 若是一个利未人不将所卖的房屋赎回，是在所得为业的城内，到了禧年就要出买主的手，因为利未人城邑的房屋是他们在以色列人中的产业。
chapters.en: And if a man purchase of the Levites, then the house that was sold, and the city of his possession, shall go out in jubile: for the houses of the cities of the Levites their possession among the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 只是他们各城郊野之地不可卖，因为是他们永远的产业。
chapters.en: But the field of the suburbs of their cities may not be sold; for it their perpetual possession.

chapters.zh: 你的弟兄在你那里若渐渐贫穷，手中缺乏，你就要帮补他，使他与你同住，像外人和寄居的一样。
chapters.en: And if thy brother be waxen poor, and fallen in decay with thee; then thou shalt relieve him: a stranger, or a sojourner; that he may live with thee.

chapters.zh: 不可向他取利，也不可向他多要；只要敬畏你的神，使你的弟兄与你同住。
chapters.en: Take thou no usury of him, or increase: but fear thy God; that thy brother may live with thee.

chapters.zh: 你借钱给他，不可向他取利；借粮给他，也不可向他多要。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not give him thy money upon usury, nor lend him thy victuals for increase.

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神，曾领你们从埃及地出来，为要把迦南地赐给你们，要作你们的神。
chapters.en: I the LORD your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, to be your God.

chapters.zh: 你的弟兄若在你那里渐渐穷乏，将自己卖给你，不可叫他像奴仆服事你。
chapters.en: And if thy brother by thee be waxen poor, and be sold unto thee; thou shalt not compel him to serve as a bondservant:

chapters.zh: 他要在你那里像雇工人和寄居的一样，要服事你直到禧年。
chapters.en: as an hired servant, as a sojourner, he shall be with thee, shall serve thee unto the year of jubile:

chapters.zh: 到了禧年，他和他儿女要离开你，一同出去归回本家，到他祖宗的地业那里去。
chapters.en: And shall he depart from thee, he and his children with him, and shall return unto his own family, and unto the possession of his fathers shall he return.

chapters.zh: 因为他们是我的仆人，是我从埃及地领出来的，不可卖为奴仆。
chapters.en: For they my servants, which I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: they shall not be sold as bondmen.

chapters.zh: 不可严严的辖管他，只要敬畏你的神。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not rule over him with rigour; but shalt fear thy God.

chapters.zh: 至于你的奴仆、婢女，可以从你四围的国中买。
chapters.en: Both thy bondmen, and thy bondmaids, which thou shalt have, of the heathen that are round about you; of them shall ye buy bondmen and bondmaids.

chapters.zh: 并且那寄居在你们中间的外人和他们的家属，在你们地上所生的，你们也可以从其中买人；他们要作你们的产业。
chapters.en: Moreover of the children of the strangers that do sojourn among you, of them shall ye buy, and of their families that with you, which they begat in your land: and they shall be your possession.

chapters.zh: 你们要将他们遗留给你们的子孙为产业，要永远从他们中间拣出奴仆；只是你们的弟兄以色列人，你们不可严严的辖管。
chapters.en: And ye shall take them as an inheritance for your children after you, to inherit a possession; they shall be your bondmen for ever: but over your brethren the children of Israel, ye shall not rule one over another with rigour.

chapters.zh: 住在你那里的外人，或是寄居的，若渐渐富足，你的弟兄却渐渐穷乏，将自己卖给那外人，或是寄居的，或是外人的宗族，
chapters.en: And if a sojourner or stranger wax rich by thee, and thy brother by him wax poor, and sell himself unto the stranger sojourner by thee, or to the stock of the stranger's family:

chapters.zh: 卖了以后，可以将他赎回。无论是他的弟兄，
chapters.en: After that he is sold he may be redeemed again; one of his brethren may redeem him:

chapters.zh: 或伯叔、伯叔的儿子，本家的近支，都可以赎他。他自己若渐渐富足，也可以自赎。
chapters.en: Either his uncle, or his uncle's son, may redeem him, or that is nigh of kin unto him of his family may redeem him; or if he be able, he may redeem himself.

chapters.zh: 他要和买主计算，从卖自己的那年起，算到禧年；所卖的价值照著年数多少，好像工人每年的工价。
chapters.en: And he shall reckon with him that bought him from the year that he was sold to him unto the year of jubile: and the price of his sale shall be according unto the number of years, according to the time of an hired servant shall it be with him.

chapters.zh: 若缺少的年数多，就要按著年数从买价中偿还他的赎价
chapters.en: If yet many years , according unto them he shall give again the price of his redemption out of the money that he was bought for.

chapters.zh: 若到禧年只缺少几年，就要按著年数和买主计算，偿还他的赎价。
chapters.en: And if there remain but few years unto the year of jubile, then he shall count with him, according unto his years shall he give him again the price of his redemption.

chapters.zh: 他和买主同住，要像每年雇的工人，买主不可严严的辖管他。
chapters.en: as a yearly hired servant shall he be with him: shall not rule with rigour over him in thy sight.

chapters.zh: 他若不这样被赎，到了禧年，要和他的儿女一同出去。
chapters.en: And if he be not redeemed in these , then he shall go out in the year of jubile, he, and his children with him.

chapters.zh: 因为以色列人都是我的仆人，是我从埃及地领出来的。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: For unto me the children of Israel servants; they my servants whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们不可做甚么虚无的神像，不可立雕刻的偶像或是柱像，也不可在你们的地上安甚么錾成的石像，向他跪拜，因为我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Ye shall make you no idols nor graven image, neither rear you up a standing image, neither shall ye set up image of stone in your land, to bow down unto it: for I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 你们要守我的安息日，敬我的圣所。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们若遵行我的律例，谨守我的诫命，
chapters.en: If ye walk in my statutes, and keep my commandments, and do them;

chapters.zh: 我就给你们降下时雨，叫地生出土产，田野的树木结果子。
