chapters.en: Then I will give you rain in due season, and the land shall yield her increase, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.

chapters.zh: 你们打粮食要打到摘葡萄的时候，摘葡萄要摘到撒种的时候；并且要吃得饱足，在你们的地上安然居住。
chapters.en: And your threshing shall reach unto the vintage, and the vintage shall reach unto the sowing time: and ye shall eat your bread to the full, and dwell in your land safely.

chapters.zh: 我要赐平安在你们的地上；你们躺卧，无人惊吓。我要叫恶兽从你们的地上息灭；刀剑也必不经过你们的地。
chapters.en: And I will give peace in the land, and ye shall lie down, and none shall make afraid: and I will rid evil beasts out of the land, neither shall the sword go through your land.

chapters.zh: 你们要追赶仇敌，他们必倒在你们刀下。
chapters.en: And ye shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.

chapters.zh: 你们五个人要追赶一百人，一百人要追赶一万人；仇敌必倒在你们刀下。
chapters.en: And five of you shall chase an hundred, and an hundred of you shall put ten thousand to flight: and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.

chapters.zh: 我要眷顾你们，使你们生养众多，也要与你们坚定所立的约。
chapters.en: For I will have respect unto you, and make you fruitful, and multiply you, and establish my covenant with you.

chapters.zh: 你们要吃陈粮，又因新粮挪开陈粮。
chapters.en: And ye shall eat old store, and bring forth the old because of the new.

chapters.zh: 我要在你们中间立我的帐幕；我的心也不厌恶你们。
chapters.en: And I will set my tabernacle among you: and my soul shall not abhor you.

chapters.zh: 我要在你们中间行走；我要作你们的神，你们要作我的子民。
chapters.en: And I will walk among you, and will be your God, and ye shall be my people.

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神，曾将你们从埃及地领出来，使你们不作埃及人的奴仆；我也折断你们所负的轭，叫你们挺身而走。
chapters.en: I the LORD your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that ye should not be their bondmen; and I have broken the bands of your yoke, and made you go upright.

chapters.zh: 你们若不听从我，不遵行我的诫命，
chapters.en: But if ye will not hearken unto me, and will not do all these commandments;

chapters.zh: 厌弃我的律例，厌恶我的典章，不遵行我一切的诫命，背弃我的约，
chapters.en: And if ye shall despise my statutes, or if your soul abhor my judgments, so that ye will not do all my commandments, that ye break my covenant:

chapters.zh: 我待你们就要这样：我必命定惊惶，叫眼目干瘪、精神消耗的痨病热病辖制你们。你们也要白白的撒种，因为仇敌要吃你们所种的。
chapters.en: I also will do this unto you; I will even appoint over you terror, consumption, and the burning ague, that shall consume the eyes, and cause sorrow of heart: and ye shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.

chapters.zh: 我要向你们变脸，你们就要败在仇敌面前。恨恶你们的，必辖管你们；无人追赶，你们却要逃跑。
chapters.en: And I will set my face against you, and ye shall be slain before your enemies: they that hate you shall reign over you; and ye shall flee when none pursueth you.

chapters.zh: 你们因这些事若还不听从我，我就要为你们的罪加七倍惩罚你们。
chapters.en: And if ye will not yet for all this hearken unto me, then I will punish you seven times more for your sins.

chapters.zh: 我必断绝你们因势力而有的骄傲，又要使覆你们的天如铁，载你们的地如铜。
chapters.en: And I will break the pride of your power; and I will make your heaven as iron, and your earth as brass:

chapters.zh: 你们要白白的劳力；因为你们的地不出土产，其上的树木也不结果子。
chapters.en: And your strength shall be spent in vain: for your land shall not yield her increase, neither shall the trees of the land yield their fruits.

chapters.zh: 你们行事若与我反对，不肯听从我，我就要按你们的罪加七倍降灾与你们。
chapters.en: And if ye walk contrary unto me, and will not hearken unto me; I will bring seven times more plagues upon you according to your sins.

chapters.zh: 我也要打发野地的走兽到你们中间，抢吃你们的儿女，吞灭你们的牲畜，使你们的人数减少，道路荒凉。
chapters.en: I will also send wild beasts among you, which shall rob you of your children, and destroy your cattle, and make you few in number; and your ways shall be desolate.

chapters.zh: 你们因这些事若仍不改正归我，行事与我反对，
chapters.en: And if ye will not be reformed by me by these things, but will walk contrary unto me;

chapters.zh: 我就要行事与你们反对，因你们的罪击打你们七次。
chapters.en: Then will I also walk contrary unto you, and will punish you yet seven times for your sins.

chapters.zh: 我又要使刀剑临到你们，报复你们背约的仇；聚集你们在各城内，降瘟疫在你们中间，也必将你们交在仇敌的手中。
chapters.en: And I will bring a sword upon you, that shall avenge the quarrel of covenant: and when ye are gathered together within your cities, I will send the pestilence among you; and ye shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.

chapters.zh: 我要折断你们的杖，就是断绝你们的粮。那时，必有十个女人在一个炉子给你们烤饼，按分量秤给你们；你们要吃，也吃不饱。
chapters.en: when I have broken the staff of your bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall deliver your bread again by weight: and ye shall eat, and not be satisfied.

chapters.zh: 你们因这一切的事若不听从我，却行事与我反对，
chapters.en: And if ye will not for all this hearken unto me, but walk contrary unto me;

chapters.zh: 我就要发烈怒，行事与你们反对，又因你们的罪惩罚你们七次。
chapters.en: Then I will walk contrary unto you also in fury; and I, even I, will chastise you seven times for your sins.

chapters.zh: 并且你们要吃儿子的肉，也要吃女儿的肉。
chapters.en: And ye shall eat the flesh of your sons, and the flesh of your daughters shall ye eat.

chapters.zh: 我又要毁坏你们的邱坛，砍下你们的日像，把你们的尸首扔在你们偶像的身上；我的心也必厌恶你们。
chapters.en: And I will destroy your high places, and cut down your images, and cast your carcases upon the carcases of your idols, and my soul shall abhor you.

chapters.zh: 我要使你们的城邑变为荒凉，使你们的众圣所成为荒场；我也不闻你们馨香的香气。
chapters.en: And I will make your cities waste, and bring your sanctuaries unto desolation, and I will not smell the savour of your sweet odours.

chapters.zh: 我要使地成为荒场，住在其上的仇敌就因此诧异。
chapters.en: And I will bring the land into desolation: and your enemies which dwell therein shall be astonished at it.

chapters.zh: 我要把你们散在列邦中；我也要拔刀追赶你们。你们的地要成为荒场；你们的城邑要变为荒凉。
chapters.en: And I will scatter you among the heathen, and will draw out a sword after you: and your land shall be desolate, and your cities waste.

chapters.zh: 你们在仇敌之地居住的时候，你们的地荒凉，要享受众安息；正在那时候，地要歇息，享受安息。
chapters.en: Then shall the land enjoy her sabbaths, as long as it lieth desolate, and ye in your enemies' land; then shall the land rest, and enjoy her sabbaths.

chapters.zh: 地多时为荒场，就要多时歇息；地这样歇息，是你们住在其上的安息年所不能得的。
chapters.en: As long as it lieth desolate it shall rest; because it did not rest in your sabbaths, when ye dwelt upon it.

chapters.zh: 至于你们剩下的人，我要使他们在仇敌之地心惊胆怯。叶子被风吹的响声，要追赶他们；他们要逃避，像人逃避刀剑，无人追赶，却要跌倒。
chapters.en: And upon them that are left of you I will send a faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; and the sound of a shaken leaf shall chase them; and they shall flee, as fleeing from a sword; and they shall fall when none pursueth.

chapters.zh: 无人追赶，他们要彼此撞跌，像在刀剑之前。你们在仇敌面前也必站立不住。
chapters.en: And they shall fall one upon another, as it were before a sword, when none pursueth: and ye shall have no power to stand before your enemies.

chapters.zh: 你们要在列邦中灭亡；仇敌之地要吞吃你们。
chapters.en: And ye shall perish among the heathen, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.

chapters.zh: 你们剩下的人必因自己的罪孽和祖宗的罪孽在仇敌之地消灭。
chapters.en: And they that are left of you shall pine away in their iniquity in your enemies' lands; and also in the iniquities of their fathers shall they pine away with them.

chapters.zh: 他们要承认自己的罪和他们祖宗的罪，就是干犯我的那罪，并且承认自己行事与我反对，
chapters.en: If they shall confess their iniquity, and the iniquity of their fathers, with their trespass which they trespassed against me, and that also they have walked contrary unto me;

chapters.zh: 我所以行事与他们反对，把他们带到仇敌之地。那时，他们未受割礼的心若谦卑了，他们也服了罪孽的刑罚，
chapters.en: And I also have walked contrary unto them, and have brought them into the land of their enemies; if then their uncircumcised hearts be humbled, and they then accept of the punishment of their iniquity:

chapters.zh: 我就要记念我与雅各所立的约，与以撒所立的约，与亚伯拉罕所立的约，并要记念这地。
chapters.en: Then will I remember my covenant with Jacob, and also my covenant with Isaac, and also my covenant with Abraham will I remember; and I will remember the land.

chapters.zh: 他们离开这地，地在荒废无人的时候就要享受安息。并且他们要服罪孽的刑罚；因为他们厌弃了我的典章，心中厌恶了我的律例。
chapters.en: The land also shall be left of them, and shall enjoy her sabbaths, while she lieth desolate without them: and they shall accept of the punishment of their iniquity: because, even because they despised my judgments, and because their soul abhorred my statutes.

chapters.zh: 虽是这样，他们在仇敌之地，我却不厌弃他们，也不厌恶他们，将他们尽行灭绝，也不背弃我与他们所立的约，因为我是耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: And yet for all that, when they be in the land of their enemies, I will not cast them away, neither will I abhor them, to destroy them utterly, and to break my covenant with them: for I the LORD their God.

chapters.zh: 我却要为他们的缘故记念我与他们先祖所立的约。他们的先祖是我在列邦人眼前、从埃及地领出来的，为要作他们的神。我是耶和华。
chapters.en: But I will for their sakes remember the covenant of their ancestors, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the heathen, that I might be their God: I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 这些律例、典章，和法度是耶和华与以色列人在西乃山藉著摩西立的。
chapters.en: These the statutes and judgments and laws, which the LORD made between him and the children of Israel in mount Sinai by the hand of Moses.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：人还特许的愿，被许的人要按你所估的价值归给耶和华。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When a man shall make a singular vow, the persons for the LORD by thy estimation.

chapters.zh: 你估定的，从二十岁到六十岁的男人，要按圣所的平，估定价银五十舍客勒。
chapters.en: And thy estimation shall be of the male from twenty years old even unto sixty years old, even thy estimation shall be fifty shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 若是女人，你要估定三十舍客勒。
chapters.en: And if it a female, then thy estimation shall be thirty shekels.

chapters.zh: 若是从五岁到二十岁，男子你要估定二十舍客勒，女子估定十舍客勒。
chapters.en: And if from five years old even unto twenty years old, then thy estimation shall be of the male twenty shekels, and for the female ten shekels.

chapters.zh: 若是从一月到五岁，男子你要估定五舍客勒，女子估定三舍客勒。
chapters.en: And if from a month old even unto five years old, then thy estimation shall be of the male five shekels of silver, and for the female thy estimation three shekels of silver.

chapters.zh: 若是从六十岁以上，男人你要估定十五舍客勒，女人估定十舍客勒。
chapters.en: And if from sixty years old and above; if a male, then thy estimation shall be fifteen shekels, and for the female ten shekels.

chapters.zh: 他若贫穷，不能照你所估定的价，就要把他带到祭司面前，祭司要按许愿人的力量估定他的价。
chapters.en: But if he be poorer than thy estimation, then he shall present himself before the priest, and the priest shall value him; according to his ability that vowed shall the priest value him.

chapters.zh: 所许的若是牲畜，就是人献给耶和华为供物的，凡这一类献给耶和华的，都要成为圣。
chapters.en: And if a beast, whereof men bring an offering unto the LORD, all that giveth of such unto the LORD shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 人不可改换，也不可更换，或是好的换坏的，或是坏的换好的。若以牲畜更换牲畜，所许的与所换的都要成为圣。
chapters.en: He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good: and if he shall at all change beast for beast, then it and the exchange thereof shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 若牲畜不洁净，是不可献给耶和华为供物的，就要把牲畜安置在祭司面前。
chapters.en: And if any unclean beast, of which they do not offer a sacrifice unto the LORD, then he shall present the beast before the priest:

chapters.zh: 祭司就要估定价值；牲畜是好是坏，祭司怎样估定，就要以怎样为是。
chapters.en: And the priest shall value it, whether it be good or bad: as thou valuest it, the priest, so shall it be.

chapters.zh: 他若一定要赎回，就要在你所估定的价值以外加上五分之一。
chapters.en: But if he will at all redeem it, then he shall add a fifth thereof unto thy estimation.

chapters.zh: 人将房屋分别为圣，归给耶和华，祭司就要估定价值。房屋是好是坏，祭司怎样估定，就要以怎样为定。
chapters.en: And when a man shall sanctify his house holy unto the LORD, then the priest shall estimate it, whether it be good or bad: as the priest shall estimate it, so shall it stand.

chapters.zh: 将房屋分别为圣的人，若要赎回房屋，就必在你所估定的价值以外加上五分之一，房屋仍旧归他。
chapters.en: And if he that sanctified it will redeem his house, then he shall add the fifth of the money of thy estimation unto it, and it shall be his.

chapters.zh: 人若将承受为业的几分地分别为圣，归给耶和华，你要按这地撒种多少估定价值，若撒大麦一贺梅珥，要估价五十舍客勒。
chapters.en: And if a man shall sanctify unto the LORD of a field of his possession, then thy estimation shall be according to the seed thereof: an homer of barley seed at fifty shekels of silver.

chapters.zh: 他若从禧年将地分别为圣，就要以你所估定的价为定。
chapters.en: If he sanctify his field from the year of jubile, according to thy estimation it shall stand.

chapters.zh: 倘若他在禧年以后将地分别为圣，祭司就要按著未到禧年所剩的年数推算价值，也要从你所估的减去价值。
chapters.en: But if he sanctify his field after the jubile, then the priest shall reckon unto him the money according to the years that remain, even unto the year of the jubile, and it shall be abated from thy estimation.

chapters.zh: 将地分别为圣的人若定要把地赎回，他便要在你所估的价值以外加上五分之一，地就准定归他。
chapters.en: And if he that sanctified the field will in any wise redeem it, then he shall add the fifth of the money of thy estimation unto it, and it shall be assured to him.

chapters.zh: 他若不赎回那地，或是将地卖给别人，就再不能赎了。
chapters.en: And if he will not redeem the field, or if he have sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed any more.

chapters.zh: 但到了禧年，那地从买主手下出来的时候，就要归耶和华为圣，和永献的地一样，要归祭司为业。
chapters.en: But the field, when it goeth out in the jubile, shall be holy unto the LORD, as a field devoted; the possession thereof shall be the priest's.

chapters.zh: 他若将所买的一块地，不是承受为业的，分别为圣归给耶和华，
chapters.en: And if sanctify unto the LORD a field which he hath bought, which not of the fields of his possession;

chapters.zh: 祭司就要将你所估的价值给他推算到禧年。当日，他要以你所估的价银为圣，归给耶和华。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall reckon unto him the worth of thy estimation, unto the year of the jubile: and he shall give thine estimation in that day, a holy thing unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 到了禧年，那地要归卖主，就是那承受为业的原主。
chapters.en: In the year of the jubile the field shall return unto him of whom it was bought, to him to whom the possession of the land .

chapters.zh: 凡你所估定的价银都要按著圣所的平：二十季拉为一舍客勒。
chapters.en: And all thy estimations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.

chapters.zh: 惟独牲畜中头生的，无论是牛是羊，既归耶和华，谁也不可再分别为圣，因为这是耶和华的。
chapters.en: Only the firstling of the beasts, which should be the LORD'S firstling, no man shall sanctify it; whether ox, or sheep: it the LORD'S.

chapters.zh: 若是不洁净的牲畜生的，就要按你所估定的价值加上五分之一赎回；若不赎回，就要按你所估定的价值卖了。
chapters.en: And if of an unclean beast, then he shall redeem according to thine estimation, and shall add a fifth of it thereto: or if it be not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to thy estimation.

chapters.zh: 但一切永献的，就是人从他所有永献给耶和华的，无论是人，是牲畜，是他承受为业的地，都不可卖，也不可赎。凡永献的是归给耶和华为至圣。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding no devoted thing, that a man shall devote unto the LORD of all that he hath, of man and beast, and of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed: every devoted thing most holy unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 凡从人中当灭的都不可赎，必被治死。
chapters.en: None devoted, which shall be devoted of men, shall be redeemed; shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 地上所有的，无论是地上的种子是树上的果子，十分之一是耶和华的，是归给耶和华为圣的。
chapters.en: And all the tithe of the land, of the seed of the land, of the fruit of the tree, the LORD'S: holy unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人若要赎这十分之一的甚么物，就要加上五分之一。
chapters.en: And if a man will at all redeem of his tithes, he shall add thereto the fifth thereof.

chapters.zh: 凡牛群羊群中，一切从杖下经过的，每第十只要归给耶和华为圣。
chapters.en: And concerning the tithe of the herd, or of the flock, of whatsoever passeth under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 不可问是好是坏，也不可更换；若定要更换，所更换的与本来的牲畜都要成为圣，不可赎回。
chapters.en: He shall not search whether it be good or bad, neither shall he change it: and if he change it at all, then both it and the change thereof shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.

chapters.zh: 这就是耶和华在西乃山为以色列人所吩咐摩西的命令。
chapters.en: These the commandments, which the LORD commanded Moses for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.
